P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t Returns the new status on success.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Legal transitions (plan §2 topology-change verbs):\n65\t    /// - (new) → Joining          (admin API: POST /_miroir/nodes)\n66\t    /// - Joining → Active         (migration complete)\n67\t    /// - Active → Draining        (admin API: POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain)\n68\t    /// - Draining → Removed       (migration complete)\n69\t    /// - Active/Draining → Failed (health check detects failure)\n70\t    /// - Failed → Active          (health check recovery)\n71\t    /// - Active/Failed → Degraded (partial health: timeouts)\n72\t    /// - Degraded → Active        (health restored)\n73\t    pub fn transition_to(self, target: NodeStatus) -> Result<NodeStatus> {\n74\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n75\t\n76\t        let legal = match (self, target) {\n77\t            // Normal lifecycle\n78\t            (Joining, Active) => true,\n79\t            (Active, Draining) => true,\n80\t            (Draining, Removed) => true,\n81\t\n82\t            // Failure detection\n83\t            (Active, Failed) => true,\n84\t            (Draining, Failed) => true,\n85\t\n86\t            // Recovery\n87\t            (Failed, Active) => true,\n88\t\n89\t            // Degraded\n90\t            (Active, Degraded) => true,\n91\t            (Failed, Degraded) => true,\n92\t            (Degraded, Active) => true,\n93\t\n94\t            // Idempotent\n95\t            (Active, Active)\n96\t            | (Failed, Failed)\n97\t            | (Degraded, Degraded)\n98\t            | (Joining, Joining)\n99\t            | (Draining, Draining) => true,\n100\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_cd024eb9e7824613be2dddd0","session_id":"4c492678-e3a6-4eb7-8039-c2c30e309afc","uuid":"4c16ae6d-db82-4b33-a08c-dd705b8cc32f","timestamp":"2026-05-23T17:59:16.073Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore codebase for search implementation"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e0e24bf11f1b4437810302be","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Distributed Search Patterns — Research for Miroir\n2\t\n3\tMiroir is a RAID-like orchestration layer for Meilisearch CE: it must distribute documents across N nodes, scatter search queries, merge ranked results, and handle topology changes without relying on Meilisearch Pro/Enterprise. This document surveys how production distributed search systems solve those same problems.\n4\t\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t## 1. Elasticsearch / OpenSearch\n8\t\n9\t### Sharding Model\n10\t\n11\tEach index is divided into a fixed number of primary shards at creation time. Shard count is **immutable** after index creation (without reindex). Documents are routed to a shard by:\n12\t\n13\t```\n14\tshard = hash(routing_value) % number_of_primary_shards\n15\t```\n16\t\n17\tThe default `routing_value` is `_id`. A custom routing key can be set per-document or per-request, enabling co-location of related documents (important for aggregations that must not cross shard boundaries).\n18\t\n19\tEach primary shard has zero or more replica shards. Replicas are placed on different nodes than their primary (enforced by the allocator). Replicas serve both read traffic and failover.\n20\t\n21\t### Cluster State and Routing Table\n22\t\n23\tThe elected master node maintains the **cluster state**: a data structure containing the routing table (which shard lives on which node), index mappings, and node membership. Every node holds a copy. The master propagates cluster state updates via a publish/acknowledge protocol — no node acts on a routing change until the master confirms a quorum of nodes has acknowledged it.\n24\t\n25\tWhen a node joins or leaves, the **ShardsAllocator** is invoked:\n26\t- Assigns orphaned primary shards to surviving nodes.\n27\t- Rebalances shard counts across nodes toward equal distribution.\n28\t- Respects constraints: no primary and its replica on the same node; allocation awareness attributes (rack, zone).\n29\t- Concurrent rebalancing is throttled (`cluster.routing.allocation.cluster_concurrent_rebalances`).\n30\t\n31\tShard recovery after failover requires replaying the translog (write-ahead log) from the last checkpoint, or copying segments from another replica.\n32\t\n33\t### Scatter-Gather at the HTTP Layer\n34\t\n35\tAny node can act as a **coordinating node** (the default role for nodes receiving client requests). The coordinating node:\n36\t\n37\t1. **Query phase**: Sends the query to one copy (primary or replica, selected by adaptive replica selection) of every relevant shard. Each shard executes the query locally against its Lucene index, producing a priority queue of (doc_id, score) pairs of size `from + size`. Returns only IDs and scores — no source documents.\n38\t\n39\t2. **Merge phase**: The coordinating node collects all priority queues, merges them globally, and selects the top `from + size` results. This requires sorting across `num_shards × (from + size)` entries, making deep pagination expensive.\n40\t\n41\t3. **Fetch phase**: The coordinating node issues a multi-get to each shard that owns one of the selected top-N documents to retrieve `_source` and highlighting. This is a second round-trip.\n42\t\n43\t4. **Response**: Assembled result sent to client.\n44\t\n45\tTotal round trips: 2 (query phase + fetch phase), plus the initial coordination overhead.\n46\t\n47\t### The Global IDF Problem\n48\t\n49\tBM25 scoring depends on IDF (Inverse Document Frequency), which is computed **per shard** by default using only that shard's local term statistics. If shards have unequal or unrepresentative document distributions, identical documents in different shards will receive different scores, breaking relevance ranking.\n50\t\n51\tTwo mitigations:\n52\t\n53\t- **`dfs_query_then_fetch`**: Adds a pre-query round trip to all shards to gather global term statistics. The coordinating node merges them and sends global IDF values alongside the query phase request. Correct scores, higher latency.\n54\t- **Single-shard indexes**: Eliminates the problem; limits horizontal scaling.\n55\t\n56\tMeilisearch CE uses its own ranking pipeline (not BM25), but a Miroir orchestration layer will face the same conceptual issue: ranking criteria computed locally on each shard node may not be globally comparable.\n57\t\n58\t### Async Task Coordination\n59\t\n60\tES/OS do not expose explicit async task management at the search layer — searches are synchronous. For index operations (reindex, force-merge), ES exposes a Task API (`GET _tasks`). Long-running ops return a `task_id`; clients poll or use `wait_for_completion=false`.\n61\t\n62\t### Topology Change Handling\n63\t\n64\t- **Node joins**: Shards are proactively rebalanced to the new node. Concurrent shard moves are rate-limited.\n65\t- **Node leaves (graceful)**: `POST _cluster/reroute` can manually move shards before the node is removed.\n66\t- **Node fails**: Master detects via missed heartbeats (default 30s). Promotes a replica to primary immediately. Replication then rebuilds the lost replica elsewhere.\n67\t- **Routing table visibility**: Clients can call `GET _cluster/state` to observe current shard placement, or use `GET _cat/shards` for a human-readable view.\n68\t\n69\t### OpenSearch Extensions\n70\t\n71\tOpenSearch adds **Segment Replication**: instead of re-indexing documents on each replica, only Lucene segment files are copied from primary to replicas. This reduces CPU on replicas and increases indexing throughput at the cost of higher network I/O. Replicas that fall behind can serve slightly stale results during catch-up.\n72\t\n73\tOpenSearch also supports **Search Replicas** (read-only, cannot be promoted to primary), allowing separate scaling of read vs. write capacity.\n74\t\n75\t---\n76\t\n77\t## 2. Typesense Cluster Mode\n78\t\n79\t### Architecture: Full Replication, No Sharding\n80\t\n81\tTypesense clusters are **not sharded**. Every node holds the entire dataset. This is a deliberate design choice: it simplifies consistency and eliminates scatter-gather complexity.\n82\t\n83\tConsensus is managed via **Raft**: one leader, all writes go to the leader and are replicated to followers before acknowledgment. Reads can be served by any node (the node that receives the request serves it locally — no cross-node fan-out).\n84\t\n85\tFault tolerance: an N-node cluster tolerates ⌊(N-1)/2⌋ failures. Minimum 3 nodes for any HA.\n86\t\n87\t### Implications\n88\t\n89\t- No scatter-gather, no result merging — each node's answer is authoritative.\n90\t- Write throughput does not scale horizontally (all writes route to leader).\n91\t- Dataset size is bounded by single-node memory (Typesense is in-memory with on-disk persistence).\n92\t- Reads scale linearly: load balancers distribute reads across all nodes.\n93\t\n94\t### What Miroir Can Learn\n95\t\n96\tTypesense's model is the **anti-pattern** for Miroir's use case. Miroir exists specifically because CE Meilisearch cannot replicate across nodes. Typesense shows that replication-without-sharding is simpler to implement but hits dataset size walls. Miroir must shard (to distribute dataset across nodes) rather than replicate (to serve the same dataset from multiple nodes), or combine both.\n97\t\n98\t### Search Delivery Network (SDN)\n99\t\n100\tTypesense Cloud's geo-distributed SDN replicates the full dataset to multiple regions. Queries route to the nearest regional node. This is a pure read-scale / latency-optimization play, not capacity scaling.\n101\t\n102\t---\n103\t\n104\t## 3. Quickwit (Tantivy-based)\n105\t\n106\t### Architecture: Stateless Searchers + Object Storage\n107\t\n108\tQuickwit is the most architecturally relevant reference for Miroir. It separates storage from compute: all index data lives on object storage (S3/GCS), and searcher nodes are **stateless**. Any searcher can handle any query for any index. No data movement is required when the searcher pool scales.\n109\t\n110\tKey components:\n111\t- **Indexers**: Consume from ingest API or Kafka, write immutable **splits** to object storage. A split is a self-contained Lucene index file bundle (UUID-identified, with a hotcache sidecar enabling ~60ms open time on S3).\n112\t- **Metastore**: Centralized catalog (PostgreSQL in production) storing split metadata: time ranges, tag filters, shard assignment.\n113\t- **Control Plane**: Schedules indexing tasks. Validates desired vs. actual indexing plans every 3 seconds; rebuilds plans every minute.\n114\t- **Searchers**: Stateless nodes. Any searcher acts as root or leaf depending on which node receives the request.\n115\t\n116\t### Scatter-Gather\n117\t\n118\t1. Root searcher queries the metastore to identify relevant splits (filtered by time range, tags, etc.).\n119\t2. Root distributes split evaluation tasks to leaf searchers using **rendezvous hashing** for split-to-searcher affinity. This maximizes cache hit rate: the same split is always sent to the same searcher, which has likely already downloaded it from S3.\n120\t3. Leaves execute the Tantivy query locally, return partial results.\n121\t4. Root merges partial results and returns the final response.\n122\t\n123\t### Topology Changes\n124\t\n125\tBecause splits are immutable and stored in object storage, adding or removing searcher nodes requires no data movement. The rendezvous hash assignment shifts, slightly changing which splits land on which searchers (cache misses on the affected splits), but the system remains correct immediately.\n126\t\n127\t### Lessons for Miroir\n128\t\n129\t- **Split/shard immutability** is a key design win: topology changes become cache-miss events, not data migration events.\n130\t- **Rendezvous hashing for caching locality** is proven in production: same key → same node under stable conditions, graceful rebalancing on change.\n131\t- **Root/leaf terminology**: any node can be either, based on who received the request. No dedicated coordinator role.\n132\t- **Metastore as source of truth**: all nodes read the same catalog; no distributed routing table to keep in sync.\n133\t\n134\t---\n135\t\n136\t## 4. ZincSearch / Zinc\n137\t\n138\tZincSearch is a lightweight Elasticsearch alternative written in Go using Bluge as the indexing library. It has **no distributed/cluster mode** — it is a single-node system. The GitHub discussion from 2021 confirms clustering was planned but not delivered; the project has since pivoted to OpenObserve (formerly Zinc).\n139\t\n140\t**Lesson for Miroir**: ZincSearch is not a model. Mentioned here for completeness.\n141\t\n142\t---\n143\t\n144\t## 5. Redis Search (RediSearch) in Cluster Mode\n145\t\n146\t### Hash Slot Distribution\n147\t\n148\tRedis Cluster partitions the keyspace into **16,384 hash slots** using CRC16. Each primary shard owns a contiguous range of slots. Slot assignment is stored in the cluster topology map, propagated via gossip protocol. Documents (Redis keys) land in a slot deterministically; the cluster topology map tells each node which slot ranges it owns.\n149\t\n150\t### Distributed Search in Redis Cluster\n151\t\n152\tRediSearch in cluster mode requires the **Redis Enterprise** version for cross-shard search coordination. OSS Redis Cluster does not support cross-slot multi-key operations transparently for search queries.\n153\t\n154\tThe coordinator pattern: a single node receives the search request, fans out to every shard, collects top-K results per shard, merges by score, and returns the global top-K. Each shard computes scores locally using its local index statistics (same IDF problem as Elasticsearch).\n155\t\n156\t### Hash Tags\n157\t\n158\tRedis supports `{tag}` in key names to force co-location of related keys in the same slot. This is used to group documents that must be queried together (e.g., for multi-key operations). The Miroir equivalent: ensuring that documents belonging to a single logical index land in predictable, co-located positions.\n159\t\n160\t### Gossip Protocol for Topology\n161\t\n162\tNodes exchange cluster state via gossip (a subset of nodes pinged every 100ms). Failure detection uses a two-step process: `PFAIL` (suspected failure by one node) → `FAIL` (quorum confirms failure). Slot reassignment after failure is manual or handled by Redis Sentinel/Cluster auto-failover.\n163\t\n164\t---\n165\t\n166\t## 6. Cross-Cutting Lessons for Miroir\n167\t\n168\t### Scatter-Gather Implementation\n169\t\n170\tAll systems implement some variation of:\n171\t1. Fan-out query to all shards (or all relevant shards, filtered by metadata).\n172\t2. Each shard returns a local top-K (IDs + scores).\n173\t3. Coordinator merges top-K lists into global top-K.\n174\t4. (Optional) Second round-trip to fetch full document bodies for the final result set.\n175\t\n176\tFor Meilisearch CE, the federated multi-search API (`/multi-search` with `federation`) handles step 3 internally when queries target the same node. For Miroir, the orchestration layer must implement step 3 across nodes.\n177\t\n178\tMeilisearch's `federationOptions.weight` allows score weighting per sub-query. Miroir can use this to normalize scores from shards with different document counts.\n179\t\n180\t### Result Merging\n181\t\n182\tTwo main strategies:\n183\t\n184\t**Score-based merging**: Collect (doc_id, score) from each shard, sort globally by score, take top-K. Requires scores to be on a comparable scale across shards. Problem: local IDF/statistics cause score inflation on small shards.\n185\t\n186\t**Rank-based merging (Reciprocal Rank Fusion)**: Each shard returns a ranked list. RRF score: `1 / (k + rank)` summed across shards for each document. Immune to score scale differences. Works well when scores are not globally normalized. Used by OpenSearch hybrid search. Recommended for Miroir because Meilisearch's ranking is opaque and shard-local.\n187\t\n188\t### Async Task Coordination\n189\t\n190\tFor index operations (document ingestion, settings changes) that must be applied across N shards:\n191\t- ES: synchronous writes with replication factor; tasks API for long ops.\n192\t- Quickwit: control plane maintains desired vs. actual state; periodic reconciliation loop.\n193\t\n194\tMiroir needs a task tracking mechanism for write operations: every write fanned out to N shards should return a global task ID; the orchestrator polls each shard's task status and aggregates into a single status for the caller.\n195\t\n196\tMeilisearch already returns a `taskUid` per operation. Miroir's task layer: fan out → collect N `taskUid` values → persist as a composite task → poll each shard → aggregate `status` (succeeded when all succeeded, failed if any failed).\n197\t\n198\t### Topology Change Handling\n199\t\n200\t| System | Node Add | Node Remove | Data Movement |\n201\t|--------|----------|-------------|---------------|\n202\t| Elasticsearch | Auto rebalance (shard migration) | Manual drain or auto | Yes — shard copies physically moved |\n203\t| Typesense | Raft replication to new node | Quorum maintained | Yes — full dataset replicated |\n204\t| Quickwit | Hash rebalance, cache misses | Hash rebalance, cache misses | No — data stays in object storage |\n205\t| Redis Cluster | Manual slot migration | Manual slot migration | Yes — slot key migration |\n206\t\n207\tFor Miroir, document distribution is determined at write time by hashing the document primary key. Adding a node changes the hash space, requiring **rebalancing**: documents on over-assigned nodes must be re-indexed to under-assigned nodes. This is the most operationally complex part of any distributed search system.\n208\t\n209\tMitigation strategies:\n210\t- **Virtual shards** (more logical shards than nodes): redistribute virtual shards to new nodes without moving individual documents; only shard-level granularity moves.\n211\t- **Rendezvous hashing**: minimizes documents reassigned when a node is added/removed; only 1/N fraction of keys reassign.\n212\t- **Read-repair / background rebalance**: keep old placement serving reads while new placement is populated; cut over after new shard is fully indexed.\n213\t\n214\t### Coordinating Node Role\n215\t\n216\tAll systems designate one node per query as coordinator (Elasticsearch calls it the coordinating node; Quickwit calls it the root node). Any node can play this role. The coordinator:\n217\t- Knows the topology (routing table or metastore query).\n218\t- Fans out the query.\n219\t- Merges results.\n220\t- Returns the response.\n221\t\n222\tFor Miroir, the orchestration layer itself is the coordinator — it sits in front of all Meilisearch nodes and implements the scatter-gather loop.\n223\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_cd024eb9e7824613be2dddd0","session_id":"4c492678-e3a6-4eb7-8039-c2c30e309afc","uuid":"7dbe5055-5dfa-46a2-80ca-04b75eff4b9f","timestamp":"2026-05-23T17:59:40.315Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore codebase for search implementation"}

Replica Groups)**:\n- `query_group()`: Round-robin selection by query sequence number\n- Each group is an independent query pool for load isolation\n- Session pinning can override group selection for read-your-writes\n\n**Covering Set Construction**:\n- For each shard, select RF replicas from the chosen group\n- Use rendezvous hashing for deterministic assignment\n- Rotate through replicas using `query_seq` for load balancing\n\n**Request/Response Types**:\n- `SearchRequest`: Search query with global IDF support\n- `PreflightRequest/PreflightResponse`: OP#4 global-IDF preflight\n- `MergedSearchResult`: Final merged response with hits, facets, metadata\n- `ScatterResult`: Intermediate scatter results with failed shard tracking\n\nThis architecture implements a sophisticated distributed search system with support for replica groups, adaptive replica selection, global IDF preflight for cross-shard score comparability, and multiple merge strategies. P2.3 Search read path acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the scatter-gather + merge + group selection implementation.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Unique-keyword search across 3 nodes returns exactly 1 hit (proves merger + fan-out correctness)\n//! - Facet counts sum correctly across shards\n//! - Paging: 5 pages of 10 = single limit=50 order, no dupes/gaps\n//! - With one node down and RF=2: search still covers all shards (tests fall-back within the group)\n//! - With one group fully down: search uses the other group; response is not X-Miroir-Degraded\n//! - X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=... stamped when a shard has zero live replicas\n\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{plan_search_scatter, MockNodeClient, SearchRequest};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, Topology};\nuse serde_json::json;\n\n/// Create a 3-node topology with 2 replica groups and RF=2.\n///\n/// Group 0: node-0, node-1\n/// Group 1: node-2\nfn make_test_topology() -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 1));\n    topo\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A1: Unique-keyword search across 3 nodes returns exactly 1 hit.\n///\n/// This proves that:\n/// - Scatter correctly fans out to all nodes in the covering set\n/// - Merge correctly deduplicates documents across shards (using RRF)\n///\n/// Note: This test simulates a document that exists on multiple shards\n/// (replicated data). RRF deduplicates by primary key.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_returns_exactly_one_hit() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1); // 3 shards, 1 group, RF=1 for simplicity\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // All three nodes return the SAME document (same primary key = \"unique-doc-123\")\n    // This simulates a document that is replicated across multiple shards\n    let response = json!({\n        \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"unique-doc-123\", \"title\": \"Unique Result\"}],\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n    });\n\n    client.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), response.clone());\n    client.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), response.clone());\n    client.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), response);\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"unique keyword xyz123\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Use RRF strategy which deduplicates by primary key\n    let strategy = miroir_core::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Should have exactly 1 hit after deduplication\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 1, \"Should deduplicate to 1 hit\");\n    assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"unique-doc-123\");\n    assert!(!result.degraded);\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A2: Facet counts sum correctly across shards.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_facet_counts_sum_correctly() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1); // 3 shards for simplicity\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Node 0 returns category facet counts\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"electronics\": 50, \"books\": 30}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Node 1 returns category facet counts (overlapping with node 0)\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 80,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"electronics\": 40, \"clothing\": 25}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Node 2 returns category facet counts\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 60,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"books\": 20, \"clothing\": 15}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into()]),\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &ScoreMergeStrategy::new(),\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    let facets = result.facet_distribution.unwrap();\n    let category = facets.get(\"category\").unwrap();\n\n    // Verify counts are summed correctly across 3 shards\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"electronics\"), Some(&90)); // 50 + 40\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"books\"), Some(&50));      // 30 + 20\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"clothing\"), Some(&40));   // 25 + 15\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A3: Paging - 5 pages of 10 = single limit=50 order, no dupes/gaps.\n///\n/// Uses RRF which deduplicates by primary key.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_no_dupes_or_gaps() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(10, 1, 1); // 10 shards, 1 group, RF=1\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        // Only 3 nodes to ensure simple routing\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i)),\n            format!(\"http://node-{}:7700\", i),\n            0,\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 10, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Each node returns unique documents - use disjoint ID ranges to avoid collision\n    // Node 0: docs 0-16, Node 1: docs 17-33, Node 2: docs 34-49\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let start = i * 17;\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for j in 0..17 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"doc-{:03}\", start + j),\n                \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", start + j),\n                \"_rankingScore\": (100.0 - (start + j) as f64) / 100.0,\n            }));\n        }\n\n        client.responses.insert(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i)),\n            json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 17,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Use RRF strategy for deduplication\n    let strategy = miroir_core::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n\n    // Fetch all 5 pages (50 total documents, 10 per page)\n    let mut all_ids = Vec::new();\n    for page in 0..5 {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: page * 10,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false,\n            body: json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n            plan.clone(),\n            &client,\n            req,\n            &topo,\n            UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n            &strategy,\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 10, \"Page {} should have 10 hits\", page);\n        for hit in &result.hits {\n            let id = hit.get(\"id\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n            all_ids.push(id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify no duplicates\n    let unique_ids: std::collections::HashSet<_> = all_ids.iter().collect();\n    assert_eq!(unique_ids.len(), 50, \"All IDs should be unique, got {}\", unique_ids.len());\n\n    // Verify all docs from doc-000 to doc-049 are present\n    for i in 0..50 {\n        let expected = format!(\"doc-{:03}\", i);\n        assert!(all_ids.contains(&expected), \"Missing document {}\", expected);\n    }\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A4: With one node down and RF=2, search still covers all shards.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_node_down_rf2_covers_all_shards() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 1, 2); // 1 group, RF=2\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Node 0 returns valid data\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"Doc 1\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Node 1 is down (timeout)\n    client.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout);\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::execute_scatter(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // With RF=2, each shard has 2 replicas. When one fails, the other succeeds.\n    assert!(result.partial, \"Result should be partial when one node fails\");\n    assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have results from surviving replicas\");\n    assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have some failed shards\");\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A5: With one group fully down, search uses the other group (fallback).\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_group_down_fallback_succeeds_not_degraded() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 1); // 2 groups, RF=1\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0\".into()), \"http://g0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1\".into()), \"http://g1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n    assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Group 0 node is down\n    client.errors.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-g0\".into()),\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout,\n    );\n\n    // Group 1 node is healthy\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-g1\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc-1\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }),\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::execute_scatter(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Fallback to group 1 should succeed completely\n    assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should provide complete results\");\n    assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A6: X-Miroir-Degraded header includes actual shard IDs.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_degraded_header_includes_shard_ids() {\n    let topo = make_test_topology();\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // One node succeeds\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc-1\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Two nodes fail, creating specific failed shards\n    client.errors.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()),\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout,\n    );\n    client.errors.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()),\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout,\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::execute_scatter(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    assert!(result.partial, \"Result should be partial\");\n    assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have failed shards\");\n\n    // Verify failed_shards contains actual shard IDs\n    let mut shard_ids: Vec<_> = result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n    shard_ids.sort();\n    assert!(!shard_ids.is_empty(), \"Should have at least one failed shard\");\n\n    // Verify we can format the header value correctly: \"shards=3,7,11\"\n    let header_value = format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids.iter().map(|id| id.to_string()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\",\"));\n    assert!(header_value.starts_with(\"shards=\"), \"Header should start with 'shards='\");\n}\n\n/// P2.3: Integration test - end-to-end search with all features.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_search_read_path_integration() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1); // 3 shards for simplicity\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Set up realistic responses with hits, facets, and scores\n    // Each node returns different documents (no overlap)\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [\n                {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"First\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.95},\n                {\"id\": \"doc-2\", \"title\": \"Second\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.85},\n            ],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 50,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"tech\": 30, \"science\": 20}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [\n                {\"id\": \"doc-3\", \"title\": \"Third\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.90},\n                {\"id\": \"doc-4\", \"title\": \"Fourth\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.80},\n            ],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 40,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 8,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"tech\": 25, \"science\": 15}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [\n                {\"id\": \"doc-5\", \"title\": \"Fifth\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.88},\n            ],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 30,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 12,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"tech\": 20, \"science\": 10}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"integration test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into()]),\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &ScoreMergeStrategy::new(),\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify results\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 5); // All 5 unique docs\n    assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 120); // Sum of all totals\n    assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 12); // Max of 10, 8, 12\n    assert!(!result.degraded); // All nodes succeeded\n\n    // Verify facets are summed correctly\n    let facets = result.facet_distribution.unwrap();\n    let category = facets.get(\"category\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"tech\"), Some(&75)); // 30 + 25 + 20\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"science\"), Some(&45)); // 20 + 15 + 10\n\n    // Verify hits are sorted by score descending\n    for i in 0..4 {\n        let score_i = result.hits[i]\n            .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n            .unwrap();\n        let score_j = result.hits[i + 1]\n            .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            score_i >= score_j,\n            \"Hits should be sorted by score descending: {} >= {}\",\n            score_i,\n            score_j\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n/// P2.3: Verify showRankingScore is injected unconditionally.\n#[test]\nfn test_show_ranking_score_injected_unconditionally() {\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n    // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n    assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&json!(true)));\n\n    // limit must be offset + limit\n    assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&json!(10)));\n\n    // offset must be 0 (coordinator coordinator.\n153\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n154\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n155\t///\n156\t/// }\n232\t\n233\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n234\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n235\t    let (effective_index, };\n249\t\n250\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n251\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n252\t        if let sync context\n496\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n497\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n498\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n499\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n500\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n501\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n502\t                        })\n503\t                    })\n504\t                },\n505\t                replica_selector_ref,\n506\t            ).await;\n507\t\n508\t            match plan_result {\n509\t                Some(p) => p,\n510\t                None => {\n511\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n512\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n513\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n514\t                        format!(\n515\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n516\t                            floor, index\n517\t                        ),\n518\t                    );\n519\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n520\t                }\n521\t            }\n522\t        } else {\n523\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n524\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n525\t        }\n526\t    };\n527\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n528\t\n529\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n530\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n531\t\n532\t    // Build search request\n533\t    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n534\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n535\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n536\t        query: body.q,\n537\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n538\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n539\t        filter: body.filter,\n540\t        facets: body.facets,\n541\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n542\t        body: rest_body,\n543\t        global_idf: None,\n544\t    };\n545\t\n546\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n547\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n548\t        search_key,\n549\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n550\t    ));\n551\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n552\t\n553\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n554\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n555\t\n556\t    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n557\t    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n558\t    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n559\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n560\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n561\t        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n562\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n563\t            query_json,\n564\t            settings_version,\n565\t        };\n566\t\n567\t        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n568\t            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n569\t                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n570\t                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n571\t            }\n572\t            Err(_) => {\n573\t                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n574\t                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n575\t                (None, None)\n576\t            }\n577\t        }\n578\t    } else {\n579\t        (None, None)\n580\t    };\n581\t\n582\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n583\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n584\t        plan,\n585\t        &client,\n586\t        search_req,\n587\t        &topo,\n588\t        policy,\n589\t        &strategy,\n590\t    )\n591\t    .await\n592\t    .map_err(|e| {\n593\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n594\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n595\t    })?;\n596\t\n597\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n598\t    drop(topo);\n599\t\n600\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n601\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n602\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n603\t    }\n604\t\n605\t    // Build response body\n606\t    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n607\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n608\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n609\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n610\t    });\n611\t\n612\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n613\t    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n614\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n615\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n616\t    }\n617\t\n618\t    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n619\t    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n620\t        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n621\t        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n622\t        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n623\t            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n624\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n625\t            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n626\t            debug!(\n627\t                index = %effective_index,\n628\t                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n629\t                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n630\t            );\n631\t        }\n632\t        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n633\t        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // Build response with optional headers\n637\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n638\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n639\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n640\t\n641\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n642\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n643\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n644\t    }\n645\t\n646\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n647\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n648\t    if current_version > 0 {\n649\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n650\t    }\n651\t\n652\t    if result.degraded {\n653\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n654\t    }\n655\t\n656\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n657\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n658\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n659\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n660\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n661\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n662\t            .join(\",\");\n663\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n664\t    } else if result.degraded {\n665\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n666\t    }\n667\t\n668\t    let response = response\n669\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n670\t        .unwrap();\n671\t\n672\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n673\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n674\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n675\t    tracing::info!(\n676\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n677\t        index = %effective_index,\n678\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n679\t        node_count = node_count,\n680\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n681\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n682\t        \"search completed\"\n683\t    );\n684\t\n685\t    Ok(response)\n686\t}\n687\t\n688\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n689\t///\n690\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n691\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n692\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n693\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n694\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n695\t    state: Arc<AppState>,\n696\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n697\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n698\t    client_requested_score: bool,\n699\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n700\t    let start = Instant::now();\n701\t\n702\t    // Extract session ID if provided\n703\t    let sid = session_id;\n704\t\n705\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n706\t    let source_ip = headers\n707\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n708\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n709\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n710\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n711\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n712\t        .trim()\n713\t        .to_string();\n714\t\n715\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n716\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n717\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n718\t        Err(e) => {\n719\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n720\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n721\t        }\n722\t    };\n723\t\n724\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n725\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n726\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n727\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n728\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n729\t                    if !allowed {\n730\t                        warn!(\n731\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n732\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n733\t                        );\n734\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n735\t                    }\n736\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n737\t                }\n738\t                Err(e) => {\n739\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n740\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n741\t                }\n742\t            }\n743\t        }\n744\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n745\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n746\t            &source_ip,\n747\t            limit,\n748\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n749\t        );\n750\t        if !allowed {\n751\t            warn!(\n752\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n753\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n754\t            );\n755\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n756\t        }\n757\t    }\n758\t\n759\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n760\t    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n761\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n762\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n763\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n764\t            match strategy {\n765\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n766\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n767\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n768\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n769\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n770\t                        sid,\n771\t                        &state.task_registry,\n772\t                        max_wait,\n773\t                    ).await {\n774\t                        Ok(true) => {\n775\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n776\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n777\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n778\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n779\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n780\t                        }\n781\t                        Ok(false) => {\n782\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n783\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n784\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n785\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n786\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n787\t                        }\n788\t                        Err(_) => {\n789\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n790\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n791\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n792\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n793\t                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n794\t                        }\n795\t                    }\n796\t                }\n797\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n798\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n799\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n800\t                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n801\t                }\n802\t            }\n803\t        } else {\n804\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n805\t            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n806\t        }\n807\t    } else {\n808\t        // No session header - normal routing\n809\t        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n810\t    };\n811\t\n812\t    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n813\t    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n814\t    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n815\t        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n816\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n817\t    })?;\n818\t\n819\t    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n820\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n821\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n822\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n823\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n824\t                &state.pod_id,\n825\t                primary_target,\n826\t                sk.generation,\n827\t            );\n828\t            sk.primary_key\n829\t        } else {\n830\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n831\t        }\n832\t    } else {\n833\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n834\t    };\n835\t\n836\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n837\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n838\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n839\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n840\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n841\t\n842\t    // Use live topology from shared state\n843\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n844\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n845\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n846\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n847\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n848\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n849\t    };\n850\t\n851\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n852\t    // should have identical shard layout)\n853\t    let plan = {\n854\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n855\t            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n856\t            primary_target = %primary_target,\n857\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n858\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n859\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n860\t            min_settings_version,\n861\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n862\t            target_count = targets.len(),\n863\t        ).entered();\n864\t\n865\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n866\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n867\t                &topo,\n868\t                0,\n869\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n870\t                state.config.shards,\n871\t                group,\n872\t                None,\n873\t            ) {\n874\t                Some(p) => p,\n875\t                None => {\n876\t                    warn!(\n877\t                        pinned_group = group,\n878\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n879\t                    );\n880\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n881\t                }\n882\t            }\n883\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n884\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n885\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n886\t                &topo,\n887\t                0,\n888\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n889\t                state.config.shards,\n890\t                primary_target,\n891\t                floor,\n892\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n893\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n894\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n895\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n896\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n897\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n898\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n899\t                        })\n900\t                    })\n901\t                },\n902\t                None,\n903\t            ).await;\n904\t\n905\t            match plan_result {\n906\t                Some(p) => p,\n907\t                None => {\n908\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n909\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n910\t                        format!(\n911\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n912\t                            floor, primary_target\n913\t                        ),\n914\t                    );\n915\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n916\t                }\n917\t            }\n918\t        } else {\n919\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n920\t        }\n921\t    };\n922\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n923\t\n924\t    // Record scatter fan-out size\n925\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n926\t\n927\t    // Build search request for primary target\n928\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n929\t        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n930\t        query: body.q.clone(),\n931\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n932\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n933\t        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n934\t        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n935\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n936\t        body: body.rest.clone(),\n937\t        global_idf: None,\n938\t    };\n939\t\n940\t    // Create node client\n941\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n942\t        search_key,\n943\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n944\t    ));\n945\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n946\t\n947\t    // Use score-based merge strategy\n948\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n949\t\n950\t    // Execute search\n951\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n952\t        plan,\n953\t        &client,\n954\t        search_req,\n955\t        &topo,\n956\t        policy,\n957\t        &strategy,\n958\t    )\n959\t    .await\n960\t    .map_err(|e| {\n961\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n962\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n963\t    })?;\n964\t\n965\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n966\t    drop(topo);\n967\t\n968\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n969\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n970\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n971\t    }\n972\t\n973\t    // Build response body\n974\t    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n975\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n976\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n977\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n978\t    });\n979\t\n980\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n981\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n982\t        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n983\t    }\n984\t\n985\t    // Build response with optional headers\n986\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n987\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n988\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n989\t\n990\t    // Add settings headers\n991\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n992\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n993\t    }\n994\t\n995\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n996\t    if current_version > 0 {\n997\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n998\t    }\n999\t\n1000\t    if result.degraded {\n1001\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n1002\t    }\n1003\t\n1004\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n1005\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n1006\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n1007\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n1008\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n1009\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n1010\t            .join(\",\");\n1011\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n1012\t    } else if result.degraded {\n1013\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n1014\t    }\n1015\t\n1016\t    let response = response\n1017\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n1018\t        .unwrap();\n1019\t\n1020\t    // Structured log entry\n1021\t    tracing::info!(\n1022\t        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n1023\t        primary_target = %primary_target,\n1024\t        target_count = targets.len(),\n1025\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n1026\t        node_count = node_count,\n1027\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n1028\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n1029\t        \"multi-target search completed\"\n1030\t    );\n1031\t\n1032\t    Ok(response)\n1033\t}\n1034\t\n1035\t/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n1036\t/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\n1037\tpub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n1038\t    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n1039\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n1040\t        if !client_requested_score {\n1041\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n1042\t        }\n1043\t    }\n1044\t}\n1045\t\n1046\t#[cfg(test)]\n1047\tmod tests {\n1048\t    use super::*;\n1049\t\n1050\t    #[test]\n1051\t    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n1052\t        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n1053\t            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private
