Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n\nuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector;\nuse tracing::{instrument, info_span, Instrument};\nuse crate::merger::{MergeInput, MergedSearchResult, MergeStrategy, ShardHitPage};\nuse crate::router::{covering_set, covering_set_with_version_floor, query_group};\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Scatter plan: the exact shard→node mapping for a search query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScatterPlan {\n    pub chosen_group: u32,\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    pub shard_to_node: HashMap<u32, NodeId>,\n    pub deadline_ms: u32,\n    pub hedging_eligible: bool,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// §15 OP#4: Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n}\n\n/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    /// - None = client write (always emitted to CDC)\n    /// - Some(\"antientropy\") = anti-entropy repair write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"reshard_backfill\") = reshard backfill write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"rollover\") = ILM rollover write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"ttl_expire\") = TTL expiration delete (suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes)\n    ///\n    /// This field is NEVER stored on documents and never returned to clients.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteResponse {\n    pub success: bool,\n    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n    pub message: Option<String>,\n    pub code: Option<String>,\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete documents by IDs.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByIdsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub ids: Vec<String>,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByFilterRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a delete operation.\npub type DeleteResponse = WriteResponse;\n\n/// Request to fetch documents with a filter (used for shard migration).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct FetchDocumentsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n    pub limit: u32,\n    pub offset: u32,\n}\n\n/// Response from a fetch documents operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct FetchDocumentsResponse {\n    pub results: Vec<Value>,\n    pub limit: u32,\n    pub offset: u32,\n    pub total: u64,\n}\n\n/// Request to get task status from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusRequest {\n    /// The task UID to query\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's task status query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// The task UID\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Current task status\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Error message if failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    /// Error type if failed\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// Convert Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n        match self.status.as_str() {\n            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\npub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n\n    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n    }\n\n    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Get task status from a node.\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        async move {\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n            results: Vec::new(),\n            limit: _request.limit,\n            offset: _request.offset,\n            total: 0,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum NodeError {\n    Timeout,\n    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n    NetworkError(String),\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SearchRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: Option<String>,\n    pub offset: usize,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub ranking_score: bool,\n    pub body: Value,\n    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n}\n\nimpl SearchRequest {\n    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n    ///\n    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n    /// data to apply pagination.\n    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n\n        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n\n        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n\n        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n\n        // Ensure query is set\n        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n        }\n\n        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n        }\n\n        body\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ScatterResult {\n    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n    pub partial: bool,\n    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub async fn plan_search_scatter(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            match selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n///\n/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    index: &str,\n    floor: u64,\n    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n\n    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n        shard_count,\n        group,\n        rf,\n        query_seq,\n        index,\n        floor,\n        version_checker,\n    )?;\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        // Filter by version floor\n        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n                version >= floor\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            // Convert Vec<&NodeId> to Vec<NodeId> for selector\n            let eligible_owned: Vec<NodeId> = eligible.iter().map(|&n| n.clone()).collect();\n            match selector.select(&eligible_owned, chosen_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n    }\n\n    Some(ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n///\n/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    pinned_group: u32,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        if replicas.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            match selector.select(&replicas, pinned_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    Some(ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter using adaptive replica selection (plan §13.3).\n///\n/// Uses EWMA-based scoring to select the best replica for each shard,\n/// falling back to round-robin for shards with no metrics data.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_adaptive(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    replica_selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group,\n                target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n                deadline_ms: 0,\n                hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        if replicas.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Use adaptive selection to pick the best replica\n        let selected = match replica_selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n            Some(node) => node,\n            None => {\n                // Fallback to round-robin if selector returns None\n                replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n            }\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\npub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n\n    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => {\n                for shard_id in shards {\n                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n        let shard_count = shards.len();\n        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n        let span = info_span!(\n            \"scatter_node\",\n            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n            address = %node.address,\n            shard_count = shard_count,\n        );\n        tasks.push(async move {\n            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n        }.instrument(span));\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n\n    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n        match result {\n            Ok(body) => {\n                for _shard_id in shards {\n                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n                }\n            }\n            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n                deadline_exceeded = true;\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n\n    match policy {\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n            }\n        }\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n\n                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n\n                    // Try each other replica group\n                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n                        }\n\n                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n                                continue;\n                            }\n\n                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n                            for node_id in replicas {\n                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                                        Ok(body) => {\n                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n                                            break;\n                                        }\n                                        Err(_) => {\n                                            continue; // Try next replica\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n\n                            if fallback_succeeded {\n                                break; // Found a working replica\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Merge fallback results\n                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n\n                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n                        shard_pages,\n                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n                        partial: false,\n                        deadline_exceeded,\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    // Some shards still failed\n                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n\n    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n    if scatter_result.partial {\n        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n\n    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n        offset: req.offset,\n        limit: req.limit,\n        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n        failed_shards,\n    };\n\n    // Span for the merge operation\n    let _span = info_span!(\n        \"merge\",\n        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n        offset = req.offset,\n        limit = req.limit,\n    ).entered();\n\n    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\npub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n    match query {\n        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n            }\n            terms\n        }\n        _ => Vec::new(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\npub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: &PreflightRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n    }\n\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => continue,\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let nid = node_id.clone();\n        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n}\n\n/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n    };\n    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n    let mut search_req = req;\n    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mock client\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MockNodeClient {\n    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n    pub write_responses: HashMap<NodeId, WriteResponse>,\n    pub fetch_responses: HashMap<NodeId, FetchDocumentsResponse>,\n    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n    pub delay_ms: u64,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.write_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            WriteResponse {\n                success: true,\n                task_uid: Some(1),\n                message: None,\n                code: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            }\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        // Return stored fetch response if available, otherwise return empty\n        let stored = self.fetch_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            FetchDocumentsResponse {\n                results: Vec::new(),\n                limit: request.limit,\n                offset: request.offset,\n                total: 0,\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Handle pagination: if offset exceeds total, return empty results\n        if request.offset as u64 >= stored.total {\n            return Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n                results: Vec::new(),\n                limit: request.limit,\n                offset: request.offset,\n                total: stored.total,\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(stored)\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let node = node.clone();\n        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n        async move {\n            if let Some(err) = error {\n                return Err(err);\n            }\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn make_test_topology() -> Topology {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            let mut node = Node::new(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")), format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"), rg);\n            node.status = crate::topology::NodeStatus::Active;\n            topo.add_node(node);\n        }\n        topo\n    }\n\n    fn make_req() -> SearchRequest {\n        SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(), query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 0, limit: 10, filter: None, facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false, body: serde_json::json!({}), global_idf: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_pure_function() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n        assert!(plan.hedging_eligible);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_group_rotation() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        assert_eq!(plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await.chosen_group, 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 2, 64, None).await.chosen_group, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_shard_mapping() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        for s in 0..64 { assert!(plan.shard_to_node.contains_key(&s)); }\n        let g0 = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        for (_, assert!(plan_search_scatter(&make_test_topology(), 0, 2, 64, None).await.hedging_eligible);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_scatter_mock() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\"}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        assert!(scatter_gather_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap().degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: None,\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: true, // Client requested scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 10\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(10)));\n\n        // offset must be 0\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body handles large offset/limit correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_pagination() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 100,\n            limit: 50,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false,\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 150 (fetch enough for coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(150)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_18b93a264bd3441194299ac2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Supports pluggable merge strategies via the [`MergeStrategy`] trait.\n4\t//! The default strategy is Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::Result;\n7\tuse serde_json::{Map, Value};\n8\tuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n9\tuse std::cmp::Ordering;\n10\t\n11\t/// Input to the merge operation.\n12\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n13\tpub struct MergeInput {\n14\t    /// One response page per node in the covering set.\n15\t    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n16\t\n17\t    /// Original offset from the client request.\n18\t    pub offset: usize,\n19\t\n20\t    /// Original limit from the client request.\n21\t    pub limit: usize,\n22\t\n23\t    /// Whether the client requested scores in the response.\n24\t    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n25\t\n26\t    /// Facet names requested (for filtering which facets to return).\n27\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n28\t\n29\t    /// Failed shard IDs (for X-Miroir-Degraded header).\n30\t    pub failed_shards: Vec<u32>,\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// Response from a single shard (node).\n34\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n35\tpub struct ShardHitPage {\n36\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n37\t    pub body: Value,\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Merged search result.\n41\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]\n42\tpub struct MergedSearchResult {\n43\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n44\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n45\t\n46\t    /// Aggregated facet distribution.\n47\t    pub facet_distribution: Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>>,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n50\t    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n51\t\n52\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds (max across covering set).\n53\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n54\t\n55\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards had errors).\n56\t    pub degraded: bool,\n57\t\n58\t    /// Failed shard IDs (for X-Miroir-Degraded header).\n59\t    pub failed_shards: Vec<u32>,\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n63\t// Merge strategy trait\n64\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n65\t\n66\t/// Pluggable merge strategy for combining shard results.\n67\t///\n68\t/// Implementations define how hits from multiple shards are combined\n69\t/// into a single globally-ranked response. The default strategy is\n70\t/// [`RrfStrategy`] (Reciprocal Rank Fusion).\n71\tpub trait MergeStrategy: Send + Sync {\n72\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards into a single response.\n73\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult>;\n74\t\n75\t    /// Strategy name (for logging and `/explain` output).\n76\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t/// Box reference to a merge strategy (for polymorphic dispatch).\n80\tpub type DynMergeStrategy = dyn MergeStrategy;\n81\t\n82\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n83\t// RRF strategy\n84\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n85\t\n86\t/// Default RRF constant k.\n87\t///\n88\t/// The value 60 is recommended in the RRF literature and used by\n89\t/// OpenSearch for hybrid search. Smaller values amplify rank\n90\t/// differences; larger values flatten them.\n91\tpub const DEFAULT_RRF_K: u32 = 60;\n92\t\n93\t/// Reciprocal Rank Fusion merge strategy.\n94\t///\n95\t/// Each document's contribution from a shard is `1 / (k + rank + 1)`\n96\t/// where rank is the 0-based position. Documents appearing in\n97\t/// multiple shards have their contributions summed. Results are\n98\t/// sorted by total RRF score descending, with deterministic\n99\t/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n101\tpub struct RrfStrategy {\n102\t    k: u32,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl RrfStrategy {\n106\t    /// Create a new RRF strategy with the given k constant.\n107\t    pub fn new(k: u32) -> Self {\n108\t        Self { k: k.max(1) }\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    /// Create with the default k=60.\n112\t    pub fn default_strategy() -> Self {\n113\t        Self::new(DEFAULT_RRF_K)\n114\t    }\n115\t\n116\t    /// Return the configured k value.\n117\t    pub fn k(&self) -> u32 {\n118\t        self.k\n119\t    }\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\timpl Default for RrfStrategy {\n123\t    fn default() -> Self {\n124\t        Self::default_strategy()\n125\t    }\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\timpl MergeStrategy for RrfStrategy {\n129\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n130\t        rrf_merge(&self.k, input)\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n134\t        \"rrf\"\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// A document with its accumulated RRF score.\n139\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n140\tstruct RRFDocument {\n141\t    /// Accumulated RRF score across all shards.\n142\t    rrf_score: f64,\n143\t\n144\t    /// Primary key for tie-breaking.\n145\t    primary_key: String,\n146\t\n147\t    /// The hit document (JSON object) from the highest-ranking shard.\n148\t    hit: Map<String, Value>,\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\timpl PartialEq for RRFDocument {\n152\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n153\t        self.rrf_score == other.rrf_score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n154\t    }\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\timpl Eq for RRFDocument {}\n158\t\n159\timpl PartialOrd for RRFDocument {\n160\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<Ordering> {\n161\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n162\t    }\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\timpl Ord for RRFDocument {\n166\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\n167\t        // Primary sort: RRF score descending (higher score = better rank)\n168\t        match self.rrf_score.partial_cmp(&other.rrf_score) {\n169\t            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n170\t                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n171\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n172\t            }\n173\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n174\t            None => {\n175\t                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n176\t                if self.rrf_score.is_nan() && !other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n177\t                    Ordering::Less\n178\t                } else if !self.rrf_score.is_nan() && other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n179\t                    Ordering::Greater\n180\t                } else {\n181\t                    Ordering::Equal\n182\t                }\n183\t            }\n184\t        }\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Merge search results using the default RRF strategy (k=60).\n189\t///\n190\t/// This is a convenience wrapper around [`RrfStrategy`] for callers\n191\t/// that don't need to customise the strategy.\n192\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n193\t    rrf_merge(&DEFAULT_RRF_K, input)\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t/// Merge search results with a specific strategy.\n197\t///\n198\t/// Use this when the strategy is selected from config or when you\n199\t/// need a non-default RRF k value.\n200\tpub fn merge_with_strategy(\n201\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n202\t    input: MergeInput,\n203\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n204\t    strategy.merge(input)\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t/// Core RRF merge implementation.\n208\tfn rrf_merge(k: &u32, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n209\t    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n210\t    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n211\t    let mut degraded = false;\n212\t\n213\t    // Collect all hits with their ranks from all shards.\n214\t    // Use a map to aggregate RRF scores for documents appearing in multiple shards.\n215\t    let mut rrf_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, RRFDocument> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n216\t\n217\t    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n218\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n219\t\n220\t        // Check for degraded response.\n221\t        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n222\t            degraded = true;\n223\t            continue;\n224\t        }\n225\t\n226\t        // Extract estimated total hits.\n227\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n228\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n229\t                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n230\t            }\n231\t        }\n232\t\n233\t        // Extract processing time.\n234\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n235\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n236\t                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n237\t            }\n238\t        }\n239\t\n240\t        // Extract hits with ranks (position in shard's results).\n241\t        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n242\t            for (rank, hit) in hits.iter().enumerate() {\n243\t                if let Value::Object(ref map) = hit {\n244\t                    let map = map.clone();\n245\t\n246\t                    // Extract primary key for deduplication.\n247\t                    let primary_key = map\n248\t                        .get(\"id\")\n249\t                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n250\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n251\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n252\t                        .to_string();\n253\t\n254\t                    // RRF contribution: 1 / (k + rank + 1)\n255\t                    // rank is 0-based, so +1 converts to 1-based position.\n256\t                    let rrf_contribution = 1.0 / ((*k as f64) + (rank as f64) + 1.0);\n257\t\n258\t                    // Aggregate RRF scores across shards.\n259\t                    use std::collections::hash_map::Entry;\n260\t                    match rrf_map.entry(primary_key.clone()) {\n261\t                        Entry::Vacant(e) => {\n262\t                            e.insert(RRFDocument {\n263\t                                rrf_score: rrf_contribution,\n264\t                                primary_key,\n265\t                                hit: map,\n266\t                            });\n267\t                        }\n268\t                        Entry::Occupied(mut e) => {\n269\t                            // Document appears in multiple shards: sum RRF contributions.\n270\t                            e.get_mut().rrf_score += rrf_contribution;\n271\t                        }\n272\t                    }\n273\t                }\n274\t            }\n275\t        }\n276\t    }\n277\t\n278\t    // Convert map to vec and sort by RRF score descending.\n279\t    let mut merged_docs: Vec<_> = rrf_map.into_values().collect();\n280\t    merged_docs.sort();\n281\t\n282\t    // Apply offset + limit.\n283\t    let skip = input.offset;\n284\t    let take = input.limit;\n285\t    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = merged_docs\n286\t        .into_iter()\n287\t        .skip(skip)\n288\t        .take(take)\n289\t        .collect();\n290\t\n291\t    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n292\t    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n293\t    for mut doc in paginated_hits {\n294\t        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested (RRF doesn't use original scores).\n295\t        if !input.client_requested_score {\n296\t            doc.hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n297\t        }\n298\t\n299\t        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n300\t        doc.hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n301\t\n302\t        hits.push(Value::Object(doc.hit));\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Merge facets.\n306\t    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n307\t\n308\t    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n309\t        hits,\n310\t        facet_distribution,\n311\t        estimated_total_hits,\n312\t        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n313\t        degraded,\n314\t        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n315\t    })\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t/// Score-based merge strategy (OP#4 global-IDF).\n319\t///\n320\t/// This merge strategy is correct **only when** the preflight phase has\n321\t/// provided global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts\n322\t/// all hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic\n323\t/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n324\t///\n325\t/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n326\t/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n327\t/// distributions.\n328\t///\n329\t/// Use with [`dfs_query_then_fetch_search`] in the scatter module.\n330\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]\n331\tpub struct ScoreMergeStrategy;\n332\t\n333\timpl ScoreMergeStrategy {\n334\t    /// Create a new score-based merge strategy.\n335\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n336\t        Self\n337\t    }\n338\t}\n339\t\n340\timpl Default for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n341\t    fn default() -> Self {\n342\t        Self::new()\n343\t    }\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\timpl MergeStrategy for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n347\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n348\t        score_merge(input)\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n352\t        \"score\"\n353\t    }\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\t/// Core score-based merge implementation (OP#4 global-IDF).\n357\t///\n358\t/// This merge strategy is correct when the preflight phase has provided\n359\t/// global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts all\n360\t/// hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic tie-breaking\n361\t/// on primary key.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n364\t/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n365\t/// distributions.\n366\tfn score_merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n367\t    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n368\t    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n369\t    let mut degraded = false;\n370\t    let mut seen_pks = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n371\t    let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n372\t\n373\t    // Collect all hits from all shards, deduplicating by primary key.\n374\t    // Dedup is needed because execute_scatter may push the same node response\n375\t    // once per shard when multiple shards map to the same node.\n376\t    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n377\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n378\t\n379\t        // Check for degraded response.\n380\t        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n381\t            degraded = true;\n382\t            continue;\n383\t        }\n384\t\n385\t        // Extract estimated total hits.\n386\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n387\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n388\t                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t\n392\t        // Extract processing time.\n393\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n394\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n395\t                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n396\t            }\n397\t        }\n398\t\n399\t        // Extract hits.\n400\t        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n401\t            for hit in hits {\n402\t                if let Value::Object(map) = hit {\n403\t                    let pk = map\n404\t                        .get(\"id\")\n405\t                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n406\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n407\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n408\t                        .to_string();\n409\t                    if seen_pks.insert(pk) {\n410\t                        all_hits.push(map.clone());\n411\t                    }\n412\t                }\n413\t            }\n414\t        }\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking.\n418\t    all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| {\n419\t        let score_a = a.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n420\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n421\t            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n422\t        let score_b = b.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n423\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n424\t            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n425\t\n426\t        // Extract primary keys for tie-breaking.\n427\t        let pk_a = a.get(\"id\")\n428\t            .or_else(|| a.get(\"pk\"))\n429\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n430\t            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n431\t        let pk_b = b.get(\"id\")\n432\t            .or_else(|| b.get(\"pk\"))\n433\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n434\t            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n435\t\n436\t        // Primary sort: score descending (higher score = better rank)\n437\t        match score_a.partial_cmp(&score_b) {\n438\t            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n439\t                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n440\t                pk_a.cmp(pk_b)\n441\t            }\n442\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n443\t            None => {\n444\t                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n445\t                if score_a.is_nan() && !score_b.is_nan() {\n446\t                    Ordering::Less\n447\t                } else if !score_a.is_nan() && score_b.is_nan() {\n448\t                    Ordering::Greater\n449\t                } else {\n450\t                    Ordering::Equal\n451\t                }\n452\t            }\n453\t        }\n454\t    });\n455\t\n456\t    // Apply offset + limit.\n457\t    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = all_hits\n458\t        .into_iter()\n459\t        .skip(input.offset)\n460\t        .take(input.limit)\n461\t        .collect();\n462\t\n463\t    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n464\t    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n465\t    for mut hit in paginated_hits {\n466\t        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested.\n467\t        if !input.client_requested_score {\n468\t            hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n469\t        }\n470\t\n471\t        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n472\t        hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n473\t\n474\t        hits.push(Value::Object(hit));\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    // Merge facets.\n478\t    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n479\t\n480\t    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n481\t        hits,\n482\t        facet_distribution,\n483\t        estimated_total_hits,\n484\t        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n485\t        degraded,\n486\t        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n487\t    })\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/// Merge facet distributions from multiple shards.\n491\t///\n492\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable ordering (deterministic serialization).\n493\tfn merge_facets(\n494\t    shard_pages: &[ShardHitPage],\n495\t    requested_facets: Option<&[String]>,\n496\t) -> Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>> {\n497\t    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n498\t\n499\t    for shard_page in shard_pages {\n500\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n501\t\n502\t        // Meilisearch uses \"facetDistribution\" for facet results.\n503\t        if let Some(Value::Object(facets)) = body.get(\"facetDistribution\") {\n504\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n505\t                // Skip if not requested (if a filter was provided).\n506\t                if let Some(requested) = requested_facets {\n507\t                    if !requested.iter().any(|f| f == facet_name) {\n508\t                        continue;\n509\t                    }\n510\t                }\n511\t\n512\t                if let Value::Object(values_map) = facet_values {\n513\t                    let merged_facet = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n514\t                    for (value, count) in values_map {\n515\t                        if let Value::Number(n) = count {\n516\t                            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n517\t                                *merged_facet.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += n;\n518\t                            }\n519\t                        }\n520\t                    }\n521\t                }\n522\t            }\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    if merged.is_empty() {\n527\t        None\n528\t    } else {\n529\t        Some(merged)\n530\t    }\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t#[cfg(test)]\n534\tmod tests {\n535\t    use super::*;\n536\t    use serde_json::json;\n537\t\n538\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n539\t    // Trait / strategy tests\n540\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n541\t\n542\t    #[test]\n543\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_default_matches_free_function() {\n544\t        let input = MergeInput {\n545\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n546\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0)],\n547\t                100,\n548\t                15,\n549\t            )],\n550\t            offset: 0,\n551\t            limit: 10,\n552\t            client_requested_score: false,\n553\t            facets: None,\n554\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n555\t        };\n556\t\n557\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n558\t        let via_trait = strategy.merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n559\t        let via_free = }\n564\t\n565\t    #[test]\n566\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_custom_k() {\n567\t        // With k=1, rank 0 gets 1/(1+0+1) = 0.5\n568\t        // With k=60, rank 0 gets 1/(60+0+1) ≈ 0.0164\n569\t        // Both should produce the same ordering for a single shard.\n570\t        let input = MergeInput {\n571\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n572\t                vec![make_hit(\"a\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"b\", 0.5, 0)],\n573\t                50,\n574\t                10,\n575\t            )],\n576\t            offset: 0,\n577\t            limit: 10,\n578\t            client_requested_score: false,\n579\t            facets: None,\n580\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n581\t        };\n582\t\n583\t        let strategy_k1 = RrfStrategy::new(1);\n584\t        let result = strategy_k1.merge(input).unwrap();\n585\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"a\");\n586\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"b\");\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    #[test]\n590\t    fn test_merge_with_strategy_dispatches() {\n591\t        let input = MergeInput {\n592\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n593\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n594\t                50,\n595\t                10,\n596\t            )],\n597\t            offset: 0,\n598\t            limit: 10,\n599\t #[test]\n611\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_k_clamped_to_one() {\n612\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(0);\n613\t        assert_eq!(strategy.k(), 1);\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n617\t    // Core merge tests\n618\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n619\t\n620\t    fn make_hit(id: &str, score: f64, shard: u32) -> Value {\n621\t        json!({\n622\t            \"id\": id,\n623\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n624\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n625\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n626\t        })\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n630\t    fn make_hit_ranked(id: &str, shard: u32) -> Value {\n631\t        json!({\n632\t            \"id\": id,\n633\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n634\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.5, // RRF ignores score, only rank matters\n635\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n636\t        })\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    fn make_shard_response(\n640\t        hits: Vec<Value>,\n641\t        total_hits: u64,\n642\t        processing_time: u64,\n643\t    ) -> ShardHitPage {\n644\t        ShardHitPage {\n645\t            body: json!({\n646\t                \"hits\": hits,\n647\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total_hits,\n648\t                \"processingTimeMs\": processing_time,\n649\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n650\t            }),\n651\t        }\n652\t    }\n653\t\n654\t    #[test]\n655\t    fn test_merge_basic() {\n656\t        let input = MergeInput {\n657\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n658\t                vec![\n659\t                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n660\t                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0),\n661\t                ],\n662\t                100,\n663\t                15,\n664\t            )],\n665\t
