#[test]\n686\t    fn test_merge_global_sort() {\n687\t        let input = MergeInput {\n688\t            shard_hits: vec![\n689\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.5, 0)], 50, 10),\n690\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 1)], 50, 10),\n691\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 2)], 50, 10),\n692\t            ],\n693\t            offset: 0,\n694\t            limit: 10,\n695\t            client_requested_score: true,\n696\t            facets: None,\n697\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n698\t        };\n699\t\n700\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n701\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 3);\n702\t\n703\t        // RRF: all docs are at rank 0 in their respective shards.\n704\t        // With equal ranks, tie-break by primary key (alphabetically).\n705\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n706\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n707\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n708\t    }\n709\t\n710\t    #[test]\n711\t    fn test_merge_rrf_rank_ordering() {\n712\t        // Test RRF: higher rank (lower position) contributes more to RRF score.\n713\t        // shard0: [low_rank_doc (rank 0), mid_rank_doc (rank 10)]\n714\t        // shard1: [high_rank_doc (rank 0)]\n715\t        // shard2: [mid_rank_doc (rank 0) - same doc appears in two shards!]\n716\t        //\n717\t        // Expected RRF scores:\n718\t        // - low_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard0)\n719\t        // - high_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard1)\n720\t        // - mid_rank_doc: 1/(60+10+1) + 1/(60+0+1) = 1/71 + 1/61 (rank 10 in shard0, rank 0 in shard2)\n721\t        //\n722\t        // mid_rank_doc should win because it appears in multiple shards.\n723\t\n724\t        let mut shard0_hits = vec![];\n725\t        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n726\t        let mut shard2_hits = vec![];\n727\t\n728\t        // Build shard0: low_rank_doc at position 0, mid_rank_doc at position 10\n729\t        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"low_rank_doc\", 0.1, 0));\n730\t        for i in 0..9 {\n731\t            shard0_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_0_{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n732\t        }\n733\t        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.2, 0));\n734\t\n735\t        // shard1: high_rank_doc at position 0\n736\t        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"high_rank_doc\", 0.3, 1));\n737\t\n738\t        // shard2: mid_rank_doc at position 0 (same doc appears again!)\n739\t        shard2_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.4, 2));\n740\t\n741\t        let input = MergeInput {\n742\t            shard_hits: vec![\n743\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 100, 10),\n744\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 100, 10),\n745\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 100, 10),\n746\t            ],\n747\t            offset: 0,\n748\t            limit: 10,\n749\t            client_requested_score: false,\n750\t            facets: None,\n751\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n752\t        };\n753\t\n754\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n755\t\n756\t        // mid_rank_doc should be first because it appears in multiple shards\n757\t        // (RRF contributions sum: rank 10 in shard0 + rank 0 in shard2)\n758\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"mid_rank_doc\");\n759\t\n760\t        // low_rank_doc and high_rank_doc both at rank 0 in their shards\n761\t        // Tie-break by primary key alphabetically\n762\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"high_rank_doc\");\n763\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"low_rank_doc\");\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    #[test]\n767\t    fn test_merge_rrf_duplicate_handling() {\n768\t        // Test that the same document appearing in multiple shards\n769\t        // gets its RRF score summed.\n770\t        //\n771\t        // doc1 appears at rank 0 in shard0 and rank 5 in shard1\n772\t        // doc2 appears at rank 0 in shard2\n773\t        //\n774\t        // RRF(doc1) = 1/(60+0+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 1/61 + 1/66\n775\t        // RRF(doc2) = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61\n776\t        //\n777\t        // doc1 should rank higher.\n778\t\n779\t        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)];\n780\t        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n781\t        let shard2_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 2)];\n782\t\n783\t        // Add filler hits to shard1 to make doc1 appear at rank 5\n784\t        for i in 0..5 {\n785\t            shard1_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_1_{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n786\t        }\n787\t        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.2, 1));\n788\t\n789\t        let input = MergeInput {\n790\t            shard_hits: vec![\n791\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n792\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n793\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n794\t            ],\n795\t            offset: 0,\n796\t            limit: 10,\n797\t            client_requested_score: false,\n798\t            facets: None,\n799\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n800\t        };\n801\t\n802\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n803\t\n804\t        // doc1 should rank higher despite lower score because\n805\t        // it appears in multiple shards and gets RRF contribution boost\n806\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n807\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n808\t    }\n809\t\n810\t    #[test]\n811\t    fn test_merge_offset_limit() {\n812\t        let input = MergeInput {\n813\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n814\t                vec![\n815\t                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n816\t                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 0),\n817\t                    make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 0),\n818\t                    make_hit(\"doc4\", 0.6, 0),\n819\t }\n836\t\n837\t    #[test]\n838\t    fn test_merge_preserves_score_when_requested() {\n839\t        // RRF doesn't use scores for ranking, but we still preserve\n840\t        // the original score field when requested.\n841\t        let input = MergeInput {\n842\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n843\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n844\t                50,\n845\t                10,\n846\t            )],\n847\t            offset: 0,\n848\t            limit: 10,\n849\t            client_requested_score: true,\n850\t            facets: None,\n851\t // Test that RRF ordering is based on rank, not score.\n864\t        // Even though doc3 has highest score, it's ranked lower in its shard.\n865\t        //\n866\t        // shard0: doc2 at rank 0 with score 0.9 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n867\t        // shard1: doc1 at rank 0 with score 0.1 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n868\t        // shard2: doc3 at rank 2 (position 2) with score 1.0 (lower rank)\n869\t        //\n870\t        // RRF scores:\n871\t        // - doc1: 1/61 (rank 0)\n872\t        // - doc2: 1/61 (rank 0)\n873\t        // - filler: 1/62 (rank 1)\n874\t        // - doc3: 1/63 (rank 2)\n875\t        //\n876\t        // Ordering: doc1, doc2 (tie-break alphabetically), filler, then doc3\n877\t\n878\t        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 0)]; // High score, rank 0\n879\t        let shard1_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)]; // Low score, rank 0\n880\t        let shard2_hits = vec![\n881\t            make_hit(\"filler\", 0.5, 2),\n882\t            make_hit(\"filler2\", 0.5, 2),\n883\t            make_hit(\"doc3\", 1.0, 2),  // Highest score, but rank 2\n884\t        ];\n885\t\n886\t        let input = MergeInput {\n887\t            shard_hits: vec![\n888\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n889\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n890\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n891\t            ],\n892\t            offset: 0,\n893\t            limit: 10,\n894\t            client_requested_score: true,\n895\t            facets: None,\n896\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n897\t        };\n898\t\n899\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n900\t\n901\t        // doc1 and doc2 both at rank 0, tie-break alphabetically\n902\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n903\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n904\t        // filler and filler2 at rank 1\n905\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler\");\n906\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler2\");\n907\t        // doc3 at rank 2, comes last despite highest score\n908\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[4].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n909\t    }\n910\t\n911\t    #[test]\n912\t    fn test_merge_strips_miroir_fields() {\n913\t        let input = limit=50 result.\n1170\t        let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n1171\t        for i in 0..50 {\n1172\t            all_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"doc{:02}\", i), (50 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0));\n1173\t        }\n1174\t\n1175\t        let input = MergeInput {\n1176\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(all_hits, 50, 10)],\n1177\t            offset: 0,\n1178\t            limit: 50,\n1179\t            client_requested_score: false,\n1180\t            facets: None,\n1181\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1182\t        };\n1183\t\n1184\t        let full_result = merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n1185\t        assert_eq!(full_result.hits.len(), 50);\n1186\t\n1187\t        // Now fetch in pages of 10 and verify they match.\n1188\t        for page in 0..5 {\n1189\t            let page_input = MergeInput {\n1190\t                shard_hits: input.shard_hits.clone(),\n1191\t                offset: page * 10,\n1192\t                limit: 10,\n1193\t                client_requested_score: false,\n1194\t                facets: None,\n1195\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1196\t            };\n1197\t            let page_result =              full_result.hits[start..end],\n1204\t                \"Page {} mismatch\",\n1205\t                page\n1206\t            );\n1207\t        }\n1208\t    }\n1209\t\n1210\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1211\t    // RRF correctness properties\n1212\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1213\t\n1214\t    #[test]\n1215\t    fn test_rrf_cross_shard_replication_boost() {\n1216\t        // Key RRF property: a document appearing in multiple shards\n1217\t        // gets a higher merged score than a single-shard document,\n1218\t        // even if it ranks lower within each shard.\n1219\t        //\n1220\t        // doc-replicated: rank 5 in shard0, rank 5 in shard1\n1221\t        //   RRF = 1/(60+5+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 2/66 ≈ 0.0303\n1222\t        // doc-single: rank 0 in shard2\n1223\t        //   RRF = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n1224\t        //\n1225\t        // doc-replicated should rank higher.\n1226\t\n1227\t        let mut shard0 = vec![];\n1228\t        let mut shard1 = vec![];\n1229\t        for i in 0..5 {\n1230\t            shard0.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler0-{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n1231\t            shard1.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler1-{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n1232\t        }\n1233\t        shard0.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 0));\n1234\t        shard1.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 1));\n1235\t\n1236\t        let shard2 = vec![make_hit(\"doc-single\", 0.99, 2)];\n1237\t\n1238\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1239\t        let result = strategy\n1240\t            .merge(MergeInput {\n1241\t                shard_hits: vec![\n1242\t                    make_shard_response(shard0, 100, 10),\n1243\t                    make_shard_response(shard1, 100, 10),\n1244\t                    make_shard_response(shard2, 100, 10),\n1245\t                ],\n1246\t                offset: 0,\n1247\t                limit: 20,\n1248\t                client_requested_score: false,\n1249\t                facets: None,\n1250\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1251\t            })\n1252\t            .unwrap();\n1253\t\n1254\t        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n1255\t        let rep_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-replicated\").unwrap();\n1256\t        let single_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-single\").unwrap();\n1257\t        assert!(\n1258\t            rep_pos < single_pos,\n1259\t            \"Replicated doc at pos {} should rank above single doc at pos {}\",\n1260\t            rep_pos,\n1261\t            single_pos\n1262\t        );\n1263\t    }\n1264\t\n1265\t    #[test]\n1266\t    fn test_rrf_immune_to_score_scale() {\n1267\t        // RRF uses rank only: scores of 0.001 vs 0.999 don't affect ordering.\n1268\t        // Two shards with wildly different score ranges should produce the\n1269\t        // same merge as two shards with uniform scores.\n1270\t        let shard_a = ShardHitPage {\n1271\t            body: json!({\n1272\t                \"hits\": [\n1273\t                    {\"id\": \"a1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.001},\n1274\t                    {\"id\": \"a2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.002},\n1275\t                ],\n1276\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n1277\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1278\t            }),\n1279\t        };\n1280\t        let shard_b = ShardHitPage {\n1281\t            body: json!({\n1282\t                \"hits\": [\n1283\t                    {\"id\": \"b1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.999},\n1284\t                    {\"id\": \"b2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.998},\n1285\t                ],\n1286\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n1287\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1288\t            }),\n1289\t        };\n1290\t\n1291\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1292\t        let result = strategy\n1293\t            .merge(MergeInput {\n1294\t                shard_hits: vec![shard_a, shard_b],\n1295\t                offset: 0,\n1296\t                limit: 10,\n1297\t                client_requested_score: false,\n1298\t                facets: None,\n1299\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1300\t            })\n1301\t            .unwrap();\n1302\t\n1303\t        // All at rank 0 or 1 — tie-break alphabetically within each rank tier.\n1304\t        // rank 0: a1, b1 → sort by id → a1, b1\n1305\t        // rank 1: a2, b2 → sort by id → a2, b2\n1306\t        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n1307\t        assert_eq!(ids, vec![\"a1\", \"b1\", \"a2\", \"b2\"]);\n1308\t    }\n1309\t\n1310\t    #[test]\n1311\t    fn test_rrf_deterministic_with_same_input() {\n1312\t        // RRF merge is a pure function: same input always produces same output.\n1313\t        let shard = make_shard_response(\n1314\t            (0..100).map(|i| make_hit(&format!(\"doc{}\", i), (100 #[test]\n1367\t    fn test_rrf_document_equality() {\n1368\t        use super::Ordering;\n1369\t        let a = super::RRFDocument {\n1370\t            rrf_score: 1.0,\n1371\t            primary_key: \"doc1\".into(),\n1372\t            hit: Map::new(),\n1373\t        };\n1374\t        let b = super::RRFDocument {\n1375\t            rrf_score: 1.0,\n1376\t            primary_key:          primary_key: \"doc2\".into(),\n1384\t            hit: Map::new(),\n1385\t        };\n1386\t        assert_ne!(a, c);\n1387\t\n1388\t        // NaN: both NaN → Equal\n1389\t        let nan_a = assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&nan_b), Ordering::Equal);\n1400\t\n1401\t        // NaN vs real: NaN is Less\n1402\t        let real = super::RRFDocument {\n1403\t            rrf_score: 1.0,\n1404\t            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n1405\t            hit: Map::new(),\n1406\t        };\n1407\t        assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&real), Ordering::Less);\n1408\t        assert_eq!(real.cmp(&nan_a), Ordering::Greater);\n1409\t    }\n1410\t\n1411\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1412\t    // Score-based merge tests (OP#4 global-IDF)\n1413\t    // test_score_merge_global_sorting() {\n1449\t        // Test that score-based merge sorts globally by score.\n1450\t        // With global IDF (simulated here by consistent scores across preflight.\n1591\t    ///\n1592\t    /// This simulates the scenario described in P12.OP4:\n1593\t    /// - Shard 0 has 1000 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 100 (df=100)\n1594\t    /// - Shard 1 has 100 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 50 (df=50)\n1595\t    ///\n1596\t    /// Without global IDF:\n1597\t    /// - Local IDF(shard 0) = log((1000-100+0.5)/(100+0.5)+1) ≈ 2.2\n1598\t    /// - Local IDF(shard 1) = log((100-50+0.5)/(50+0.5)+1) ≈ 0.7\n1599\t    ///\n1600\t    /// With global IDF:\n1601\t    /// - Global N = 1100, global df = 150\n1602\t    /// - Global IDF = log((1100-150+0.5)/(150+0.5)+1) ≈ 1.8\n1603\t    ///\n1604\t    /// A document with tf=3 for \"rust\" in each shard:\n1605\t    /// - Shard 0 score (local IDF): ~3 * 2.2 = 6.6\n1606\t    /// - Shard 1 score (local IDF): ~3 * 0.7 = 2.1\n1607\t    ///\n1608\t    /// Without global IDF, shard 0 doc ranks higher despite shard 1 having\n1609\t    /// much higher term density (50/100 vs 100/1000).\n1610\t    ///\n1611\t    /// With global IDF, both shards use IDF=1.8, and the doc with higher\n1612\t    /// term density (normalized by document length) ranks correctly.\n1613\t    #[test]\n1614\t    fn test_score_merge_skewed_corpus_integration() {\n1615\t        // Simulate global IDF applied (scores are now comparable)\n1616\t        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n1617\t\n1618\t        // Doc in large shard with high term frequency but low density\n1619\t        let doc_large_shard = json!({\n1620\t            \"id\": \"doc-large\",\n1621\t            \"title\": \"Rust in Large Shard\",\n1622\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.75, // After global-IDF normalization\n1623\t        });\n1624\t\n1625\t        // Doc in small shard with lower term frequency but high density\n1626\t        let doc_small_shard = json!({\n1627\t            \"id\": \"doc-small\",\n1628\t            \"title\": \"Rust in Small Shard\",\n1629\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.85, // After global-IDF normalization\n1630\t        });\n1631\t\n1632\t        // With global IDF, the small shard doc should rank higher\n1633\t        // because its term density is higher (50/100 vs 100/1000)\n1634\t        let input = MergeInput {\n1635\t            shard_hits: vec![\n1636\t                ShardHitPage {\n1637\t                    body: json!({\n1638\t                        \"hits\": [doc_large_shard],\n1639\t                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1000,\n1640\t                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n1641\t                    }),\n1642\t                },\n1643\t                ShardHitPage {\n1644\t                    body: json!({\n1645\t                        \"hits\": [doc_small_shard],\n1646\t                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n1647\t                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1648\t                    }),\n1649\t                },\n1650\t            ],\n1651\t            offset: 0,\n1652\t            limit: 10,\n1653\t            client_requested_score: true,\n1654\t            facets: None,\n1655\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1656\t        };\n1657\t\n1658\t        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n1659\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n1660\t\n1661\t        // Small shard doc should rank first (higher score after global IDF)\n1662\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-small\");\n1663\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-large\");\n1664\t\n1665\t        // Scores should be preserved\n1666\t        assert_eq!(\n1667\t            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n1668\t            Some(0.85)\n1669\t        );\n1670\t        assert_eq!(\n1671\t            result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n1672\t            Some(0.75)\n1673\t        );\n1674\t    }\n1675\t\n1676\t    /// Test that demonstrates the failure mode without global IDF.\n1677\t    ///\n1678\t    /// This shows what happens when scores are NOT comparable across shards:\n1679\t    /// score-based merge produces incorrect rankings, while RRF at least\n1680\t    /// produces consistent (though not optimal) results.\n1681\t    #[test]\n1682\t    fn test_score_merge_without_global_idf_fails() {\n1683\t        // Simulate the bug: shard-local IDF produces incomparable scores\n1684\t        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n1685\t\n1686\t        // Shard 0: large shard, inflated local IDF (shard has term rarity)\n1687\t        let doc_shard0 = json!({\n1688\t            \"id\": \"doc-inflated\",\n1689\t            \"title\": \"Document               }),\n1708\t                },\n1709\t                ShardHitPage {\n1710\t                    body: json!({\n1711\t                        \"hits\": [doc_shard1],\n1712\t                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n1713\t                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1714\t                    }),\n1715\t                },\n1716\t            ],\n1717\t            offset: let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n1725\t\n1726\t        // Without global IDF, score-based merge trusts the inflated scores\n1727\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), assert_eq!(via_default.name(), via_constructor.name());\n1741\t    }\n1742\t\n1743\t    #[test]\n1744\t    fn test_score_merge_empty_input() {\n1745\t        let strategy =       assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 0);\n1757\t        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 0);\n1758\t    }\n1759\t\n1760\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1761\t    // P12.OP4 RRF skew validation\n1762\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1763\t\n1764\t    /// Validates the P12.OP4 finding: RRF merge with extreme shard skew\n1765\t    /// produces incorrect global rankings because it gives equal weight\n1766\t    /// to all shards regardless of their size.\n1767\t    ///\n1768\t    /// Scenario: 10 shards where shard 0 has 93K docs (93%) and shard 9\n1769\t    /// has 10 docs (0.01%). RRF assigns identical scores to rank-0 hits\n1770\t    /// from all shards, so a mediocre hit from the tiny shard ranks\n1771\t    /// equally with the best hit from the dominant shard.\n1772\t    ///\n1773\t    /// Benchmark result (10K queries, skewed corpus):\n1774\t    ///   Score merge:   τ = 0.79  (95% CI [0.787, 0.801]) — FAIL\n1775\t    ///   RRF merge:     τ = 0.14  (95% CI [0.134, 0.140]) — FAIL\n1776\t    ///   DFS preflight: τ = 0.98  (95% CI [0.982, 0.982]) — PASS\n1777\t    ///\n1778\t    /// Conclusion: RRF alone does NOT solve cross-shard comparability.\n1779\t    /// Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch) is required.\n1780\t    #[test]\n1781\t    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_equal_weight_problem() {\n1782\t        // Shard 0 (dominant): doc-best should be the global #1 result.\n1783\t        // It has the highest score and appears in the shard with 93% of docs.\n1784\t        let shard_dominant = make_shard_response(\n1785\t            vec![\n1786\t                make_hit(\"doc-best\", 0.95, 0),      // True global #1\n1787\t                make_hit(\"doc-good\", 0.90, 0),       // True global #2\n1788\t                make_hit(\"doc-ok\", 0.85, 0),         // True global #3\n1789\t                make_hit(\"doc-mediocre\", 0.70, 0),   // True global #4\n1790\t                make_hit(\"doc-weak\", 0.60, 0),       // True global #5\n1791\t            ],\n1792\t            93_000,\n1793\t            10,\n1794\t        );\n1795\t\n1796\t        // Shard 9 (tiny, 10 docs): due to local IDF skew, irrelevant docs\n1797\t        // can appear at rank 0 with inflated local scores.\n1798\t        let shard_tiny = make_shard_response(\n1799\t            vec![\n1800\t                make_hit(\"doc-irrelevant\", 0.98, 9), // Inflated local IDF → high score\n1801\t                make_hit(\"doc-noise\", 0.92, 9),\n1802\t            ],\n1803\t            10,\n1804\t            2,\n1805\t        );\n1806\t\n1807\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1808\t        let result = strategy\n1809\t            .merge(MergeInput {\n1810\t                shard_hits: vec![shard_dominant, shard_tiny],\n1811\t                offset: 0,\n1812\t                limit: 10,\n1813\t                client_requested_score: true,\n1814\t                facets: None,\n1815\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1816\t            })\n1817\t            .unwrap();\n1818\t\n1819\t        let ids: Vec<_> = result\n1820\t            .hits\n1821\t            .iter()\n1822\t            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()))\n1823\t            .collect();\n1824\t\n1825\t        // RRF gives equal rank weight to both shards.\n1826\t        // Rank 0 from dominant shard: 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n1827\t        // Rank 0 from tiny shard:     1/61 ≈ 0.0164 (identical!)\n1828\t        //\n1829\t        // Tie-breaking falls to primary key (alphabetical), NOT relevance.\n1830\t        // doc-best and doc-irrelevant both get RRF score 1/61.\n1831\t        // Alphabetically: doc-best < doc-irrelevant → doc-best wins the tie.\n1832\t        //\n1833\t        // But doc-irrelevant still ranks above doc-good, doc-ok, doc-mediocre,\n1834\t        // and doc-weak — all of which are more relevant globally.\n1835\t        assert_eq!(ids[0], \"doc-best\");      // Tie-break win (alphabetical)\n1836\t        assert_eq!(ids[1], \"doc-irrelevant\"); // Tie-break loss, but still rank 2!\n1837\t\n1838\t        // doc-irrelevant (globally irrelevant) ranks ABOVE doc-good (global #2)\n1839\t        let irrelevant_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-irrelevant\").unwrap();\n1840\t        let good_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-good\").unwrap();\n1841\t        assert!(\n1842\t            irrelevant_pos < good_pos,\n1843\t            \"RRF skew bug: irrelevant doc (pos {}) ranks above doc-good (pos {})\",\n1844\t            irrelevant_pos,\n1845\t            good_pos,\n1846\t        );\n1847\t    }\n1848\t\n1849\t    /// Computes Kendall tau between two rankings (document ID lists).\n1850\t    /// Used to validate merge quality against ground truth.\n1851\t    fn kendall_tau(ranking1: &[String], ranking2: &[String]) -> f64 {\n1852\t        let pos1: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking1\n1853\t            .iter()\n1854\t            .enumerate()\n1855\t            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n1856\t            .collect();\n1857\t        let pos2: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking2\n1858\t            .iter()\n1859\t            .enumerate()\n1860\t            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n1861\t            .collect();\n1862\t\n1863\t        let common: Vec<&str> = pos1\n1864\t            .keys()\n1865\t            .filter(|k| pos2.contains_key(*k))\n1866\t            .map(|k| *k)\n1867\t            .collect();\n1868\t\n1869\t        if common.len() < 2 {\n1870\t            return 0.0;\n1871\t        }\n1872\t\n1873\t        let r2_positions: Vec<usize> = common.iter().map(|id| pos2[id]).collect();\n1874\t        let (_, discordant) = count_inversions(&r2_positions);\n1875\t        let n = common.len();\n1876\t        let total = n * (n - 1) / 2;\n1877\t        let concordant = total - discordant;\n1878\t        (concordant as f64 - discordant as f64) / total as f64\n1879\t    }\n1880\t\n1881\t    fn count_inversions(arr: &[usize]) -> (Vec<usize>, usize) {\n1882\t        if arr.len() <= 1 {\n1883\t            return (arr.to_vec(), 0);\n1884\t        }\n1885\t        let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n1886\t        let (left, inv_l) = count_inversions(&arr[..mid]);\n1887\t        let (right, inv_r) = count_inversions(&arr[mid..]);\n1888\t\n1889\t        let mut merged = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());\n1890\t        let mut inv = inv_l + inv_r;\n1891\t        let (mut i, mut j) = (0, 0);\n1892\t\n1893\t        while i < left.len() && j < right.len() {\n1894\t            if left[i] <= right[j] {\n1895\t                merged.push(left[i]);\n1896\t                i += 1;\n1897\t            } else {\n1898\t                merged.push(right[j]);\n1899\t                inv += left.len() - i;\n1900\t                j += 1;\n1901\t            }\n1902\t        }\n1903\t        merged.extend_from_slice(&left[i..]);\n1904\t        merged.extend_from_slice(&right[j..]);\n1905\t        (merged, inv)\n1906\t    }\n1907\t\n1908\t    /// End-to-end validation: RRF merge on skewed shards produces τ < 0.95\n1909\t    /// against ground truth (single-index ranking).\n1910\t    ///\n1911\t    /// This is a scaled-down version of the 10K-query Python benchmark.\n1912\t    #[test]\n1913\t    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_tau_below_threshold() {\n1914\t        let k = DEFAULT_RRF_K;\n1915\t\n1916\t        // Build 5 shards with skewed sizes: [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000]\n1917\t        // Ground truth: all 17600 docs in one index, sorted by score.\n1918\t        let mut all_docs: Vec<(String, f64)> = Vec::new();\n1919\t        let mut shard_docs: Vec<Vec<(String, f64)>> = vec![vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![]];\n1920\t        let shard_sizes = [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000];\n1921\t\n1922\t        let mut rng = simple_rng(42);\n1923\t        for (shard_id, &size) in shard_sizes.iter().enumerate() {\n1924\t            for i in 0..size {\n1925\t                // Deterministic pseudo-random scores\n1926\t                let score = fake_bm25_score(shard_id, i, &mut rng);\n1927\t                let doc_id = format!(\"s{}-d{:06}\", shard_id, i);\n1928\t                all_docs.push((doc_id.clone(), score));\n1929\t                shard_docs[shard_id].push((doc_id, score));\n1930\t            }\n1931\t        }\n1932\t\n1933\t        // Ground truth: global sort by score descending\n1934\t        all_docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n1935\t        let ground_truth: Vec<String> = all_docs.iter().take(100).map(|(id, _)| id.clone()).collect();\n1936\t\n1937\t        // Per-shard: sort locally (simulates local BM25 with local IDF)\n1938\t        for docs in &mut shard_docs {\n1939\t            docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n1940\t        }\n1941\t\n1942\t        // RRF merge using the actual Rust merger\n1943\t        let shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_docs\n1944\t            .iter()\n1945\t            .map(|docs| {\n1946\t                let hits: Vec<Value> = docs\n1947\t                    .iter()\n1948\t                    .take(200)\n1949\t                    .map(|(id, score)| {\n1950\t                        json!({\n1951\t                            \"id\": id,\n1952\t                            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n1953\t                        })\n1954\t                    })\n1955\t                    .collect();\n1956\t                ShardHitPage {\n1957\t                    body: json!({\n1958\t                        \"hits\": hits,\n1959\t                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": docs.len(),\n1960\t                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n1961\t                    }),\n1962\t                }\n1963\t            })\n1964\t            .collect();\n1965\t\n1966\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(k);\n1967\t        let result = strategy\n1968\t            .merge(MergeInput {\n1969\t                shard_hits: shard_pages,\n1970\t                offset: 0,\n1971\t                limit: 100,\n1972\t                client_requested_score: true,\n1973\t                facets: None,\n1974\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1975\t            })\n1976\t            .unwrap();\n1977\t\n1978\t        let rrf_ranking: Vec<String> = result\n1979\t            .hits\n1980\t            .iter()\n1981\t            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from))\n1982\t            .collect();\n1983\t\n1984\t        let tau = kendall_tau(&ground_truth, &rrf_ranking);\n1985\t\n1986\t        // RRF with skewed shards should produce τ well below 0.95.\n1987\t        assert!(\n1988\t            tau < 0.95,\n1989\t            \"RRF tau = {:.4}, expected < 0.95 with skewed shards\",\n1990\t            tau,\n1991\t        );\n1992\t    }\n1993\t\n1994\t    /// Simple deterministic PRNG for reproducible test scores.\n1995\t    fn simple_rng(seed: u64) -> impl FnMut() -> f64 {\n1996\t        let mut state = seed;\n1997\t        move || {\n1998\t            state = state.wrapping_mul(6_364_136_223_846_793_005).wrapping_add(1);\n1999\t            (state >> 33) as f64 / (1u64 << 31) as f64\n2000\t        }\n2001\t    }\n2002\t\n2003\t    /// Simulates a BM25-like score with shard-dependent IDF skew.\n2004\t    fn fake_bm25_score(shard_id: usize, _doc_idx: usize, rng: &mut impl FnMut() -> f64) -> f64 {\n2005\t        let tf = 1.0 + rng() * 10.0;\n2006\t        // Larger shards have lower IDF for common terms (simulating skew)\n2007\t        let shard_weight = match shard_id {\n2008\t            0 => 0.3,\n2009\t            1 => 0.5,\n2010\t            2 => 0.7,\n2011\t            3 => 0.9,\n2012\t            4 => 1.0,\n2013\t            _ => 0.5,\n2014\t        };\n2015\t        tf * shard_weight + rng() * 0.5\n2016\t    }\n2017\t}\n2018\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c492678-e3a6-4eb7-8039-c2c30e309afc","uuid":"a1cfa29f-870a-48ae-84ad-36f9a0ef2dd9","timestamp":"2026-05-23T18:00:21.289Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs","content":"//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n//!\n//! Supports pluggable merge strategies via the [`MergeStrategy`] trait.\n//! The default strategy is Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde_json::{Map, Value};\nuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\nuse std::cmp::Ordering;\n\n/// Input to the merge operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MergeInput {\n    /// One response page per node in the covering set.\n    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n\n    /// Original offset from the client request.\n    pub offset: usize,\n\n    /// Original limit from the client request.\n    pub limit: usize,\n\n    /// Whether the client requested scores in the response.\n    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n\n    /// Facet names requested (for filtering which facets to return).\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n\n    /// Failed shard IDs (for X-Miroir-Degraded header).\n    pub failed_shards: Vec<u32>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single shard (node).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ShardHitPage {\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]\npub struct MergedSearchResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facet distribution.\n    pub facet_distribution: Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>>,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds (max across covering set).\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards had errors).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n\n    /// Failed shard IDs (for X-Miroir-Degraded header).\n    pub failed_shards: Vec<u32>,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Merge strategy trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Pluggable merge strategy for combining shard results.\n///\n/// Implementations define how hits from multiple shards are combined\n/// into a single globally-ranked response. The default strategy is\n/// [`RrfStrategy`] (Reciprocal Rank Fusion).\npub trait MergeStrategy: Send + Sync {\n    /// Merge search results from multiple shards into a single response.\n    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult>;\n\n    /// Strategy name (for logging and `/explain` output).\n    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;\n}\n\n/// Box reference to a merge strategy (for polymorphic dispatch).\npub type DynMergeStrategy = dyn MergeStrategy;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// RRF strategy\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Default RRF constant k.\n///\n/// The value 60 is recommended in the RRF literature and used by\n/// OpenSearch for hybrid search. Smaller values amplify rank\n/// differences; larger values flatten them.\npub const DEFAULT_RRF_K: u32 = 60;\n\n/// Reciprocal Rank Fusion merge strategy.\n///\n/// Each document's contribution from a shard is `1 / (k + rank + 1)`\n/// where rank is the 0-based position. Documents appearing in\n/// multiple shards have their contributions summed. Results are\n/// sorted by total RRF score descending, with deterministic\n/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct RrfStrategy {\n    k: u32,\n}\n\nimpl RrfStrategy {\n    /// Create a new RRF strategy with the given k constant.\n    pub fn new(k: u32) -> Self {\n        Self { k: k.max(1) }\n    }\n\n    /// Create with the default k=60.\n    pub fn default_strategy() -> Self {\n        Self::new(DEFAULT_RRF_K)\n    }\n\n    /// Return the configured k value.\n    pub fn k(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.k\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for RrfStrategy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::default_strategy()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MergeStrategy for RrfStrategy {\n    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n        rrf_merge(&self.k, input)\n    }\n\n    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        \"rrf\"\n    }\n}\n\n/// A document with its accumulated RRF score.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct RRFDocument {\n    /// Accumulated RRF score across all shards.\n    rrf_score: f64,\n\n    /// Primary key for tie-breaking.\n    primary_key: String,\n\n    /// The hit document (JSON object) from the highest-ranking shard.\n    hit: Map<String, Value>,\n}\n\nimpl PartialEq for RRFDocument {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        self.rrf_score == other.rrf_score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Eq for RRFDocument {}\n\nimpl PartialOrd for RRFDocument {\n    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<Ordering> {\n        Some(self.cmp(other))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Ord for RRFDocument {\n    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\n        // Primary sort: RRF score descending (higher score = better rank)\n        match self.rrf_score.partial_cmp(&other.rrf_score) {\n            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n            None => {\n                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n                if self.rrf_score.is_nan() && !other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n                    Ordering::Less\n                } else if !self.rrf_score.is_nan() && other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n                    Ordering::Greater\n                } else {\n                    Ordering::Equal\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Merge search results using the default RRF strategy (k=60).\n///\n/// This is a convenience wrapper around [`RrfStrategy`] for callers\n/// that don't need to customise the strategy.\npub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    rrf_merge(&DEFAULT_RRF_K, input)\n}\n\n/// Merge search results with a specific strategy.\n///\n/// Use this when the strategy is selected from config or when you\n/// need a non-default RRF k value.\npub fn merge_with_strategy(\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n    input: MergeInput,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    strategy.merge(input)\n}\n\n/// Core RRF merge implementation.\nfn rrf_merge(k: &u32, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded = false;\n\n    // Collect all hits with their ranks from all shards.\n    // Use a map to aggregate RRF scores for documents appearing in multiple shards.\n    let mut rrf_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, RRFDocument> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n        let body = &shard_page.body;\n\n        // Check for degraded response.\n        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n            degraded = true;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Extract estimated total hits.\n        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Extract processing time.\n        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Extract hits with ranks (position in shard's results).\n        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n            for (rank, hit) in hits.iter().enumerate() {\n                if let Value::Object(ref map) = hit {\n                    let map = map.clone();\n\n                    // Extract primary key for deduplication.\n                    let primary_key = map\n                        .get(\"id\")\n                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                        .to_string();\n\n                    // RRF contribution: 1 / (k + rank + 1)\n                    // rank is 0-based, so +1 converts to 1-based position.\n                    let rrf_contribution = 1.0 / ((*k as f64) + (rank as f64) + 1.0);\n\n                    // Aggregate RRF scores across shards.\n                    use std::collections::hash_map::Entry;\n                    match rrf_map.entry(primary_key.clone()) {\n                        Entry::Vacant(e) => {\n                            e.insert(RRFDocument {\n                                rrf_score: rrf_contribution,\n                                primary_key,\n                                hit: map,\n                            });\n                        }\n                        Entry::Occupied(mut e) => {\n                            // Document appears in multiple shards: sum RRF contributions.\n                            e.get_mut().rrf_score += rrf_contribution;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert map to vec and sort by RRF score descending.\n    let mut merged_docs: Vec<_> = rrf_map.into_values().collect();\n    merged_docs.sort();\n\n    // Apply offset + limit.\n    let skip = input.offset;\n    let take = input.limit;\n    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = merged_docs\n        .into_iter()\n        .skip(skip)\n        .take(take)\n        .collect();\n\n    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n    for mut doc in paginated_hits {\n        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested (RRF doesn't use original scores).\n        if !input.client_requested_score {\n            doc.hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n\n        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n        doc.hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n\n        hits.push(Value::Object(doc.hit));\n    }\n\n    // Merge facets.\n    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n\n    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n        hits,\n        facet_distribution,\n        estimated_total_hits,\n        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n        degraded,\n        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Score-based merge strategy (OP#4 global-IDF).\n///\n/// This merge strategy is correct **only when** the preflight phase has\n/// provided global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts\n/// all hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic\n/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n///\n/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n/// distributions.\n///\n/// Use with [`dfs_query_then_fetch_search`] in the scatter module.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]\npub struct ScoreMergeStrategy;\n\nimpl ScoreMergeStrategy {\n    /// Create a new score-based merge strategy.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MergeStrategy for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n        score_merge(input)\n    }\n\n    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        \"score\"\n    }\n}\n\n/// Core score-based merge implementation (OP#4 global-IDF).\n///\n/// This merge strategy is correct when the preflight phase has provided\n/// global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts all\n/// hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic tie-breaking\n/// on primary key.\n///\n/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n/// distributions.\nfn score_merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded = false;\n    let mut seen_pks = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n    let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n\n    // Collect all hits from all shards, deduplicating by primary key.\n    // Dedup is needed because execute_scatter may push the same node response\n    // once per shard when multiple shards map to the same node.\n    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n        let body = &shard_page.body;\n\n        // Check for degraded response.\n        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n            degraded = true;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Extract estimated total hits.\n        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Extract processing time.\n        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Extract hits.\n        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n            for hit in hits {\n                if let Value::Object(map) = hit {\n                    let pk = map\n                        .get(\"id\")\n                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                        .to_string();\n                    if seen_pks.insert(pk) {\n                        all_hits.push(map.clone());\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking.\n    all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| {\n        let score_a = a.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n        let score_b = b.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n        // Extract primary keys for tie-breaking.\n        let pk_a = a.get(\"id\")\n            .or_else(|| a.get(\"pk\"))\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n        let pk_b = b.get(\"id\")\n            .or_else(|| b.get(\"pk\"))\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n        // Primary sort: score descending (higher score = better rank)\n        match score_a.partial_cmp(&score_b) {\n            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n                pk_a.cmp(pk_b)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n            None => {\n                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n                if score_a.is_nan() && !score_b.is_nan() {\n                    Ordering::Less\n                } else if !score_a.is_nan() && score_b.is_nan() {\n                    Ordering::Greater\n                } else {\n                    Ordering::Equal\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Apply offset + limit.\n    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = all_hits\n        .into_iter()\n        .skip(input.offset)\n        .take(input.limit)\n        .collect();\n\n    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n    for mut hit in paginated_hits {\n        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested.\n        if !input.client_requested_score {\n            hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n\n        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n        hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n\n        hits.push(Value::Object(hit));\n    }\n\n    // Merge facets.\n    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n\n    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n        hits,\n        facet_distribution,\n        estimated_total_hits,\n        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n        degraded,\n        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Merge facet distributions from multiple shards.\n///\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable ordering (deterministic serialization).\nfn merge_facets(\n    shard_pages: &[ShardHitPage],\n    requested_facets: Option<&[String]>,\n) -> Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>> {\n    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n\n    for shard_page in shard_pages {\n        let body = &shard_page.body;\n\n        // Meilisearch uses \"facetDistribution\" for facet results.\n        if let Some(Value::Object(facets)) = body.get(\"facetDistribution\") {\n            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n                // Skip if not requested (if a filter was provided).\n                if let Some(requested) = requested_facets {\n                    if !requested.iter().any(|f| f == facet_name) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let Value::Object(values_map) = facet_values {\n                    let merged_facet = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n                    for (value, count) in values_map {\n                        if let Value::Number(n) = count {\n                            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n                                *merged_facet.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += n;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if merged.is_empty() {\n        None\n    } else {\n        Some(merged)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Trait / strategy tests\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_strategy_default_matches_free_function() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0)],\n                100,\n                15,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let via_trait = strategy.merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        let via_free = merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(via_trait.hits, via_free.hits);\n        assert_eq!(via_trait.estimated_total_hits, via_free.estimated_total_hits);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_strategy_custom_k() {\n        // With k=1, rank 0 gets 1/(1+0+1) = 0.5\n        // With k=60, rank 0 gets 1/(60+0+1) ≈ 0.0164\n        // Both should produce the same ordering for a single shard.\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"a\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"b\", 0.5, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let strategy_k1 = RrfStrategy::new(1);\n        let result = strategy_k1.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"a\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"b\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_with_strategy_dispatches() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = merge_with_strategy(&strategy, input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(strategy.name(), \"rrf\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_strategy_k_clamped_to_one() {\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(strategy.k(), 1);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Core merge tests\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    fn make_hit(id: &str, score: f64, shard: u32) -> Value {\n        json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n        })\n    }\n\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    fn make_hit_ranked(id: &str, shard: u32) -> Value {\n        json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.5, // RRF ignores score, only rank matters\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn make_shard_response(\n        hits: Vec<Value>,\n        total_hits: u64,\n        processing_time: u64,\n    ) -> ShardHitPage {\n        ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total_hits,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": processing_time,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n            }),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_basic() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                15,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 100);\n        assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 15);\n        assert!(!result.degraded);\n\n        // Score should be stripped.\n        let hit = &result.hits[0];\n        assert!(hit.get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n        assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n        assert_eq!(hit.get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_global_sort() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.5, 0)], 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 1)], 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 2)], 50, 10),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 3);\n\n        // RRF: all docs are at rank 0 in their respective shards.\n        // With equal ranks, tie-break by primary key (alphabetically).\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_rrf_rank_ordering() {\n        // Test RRF: higher rank (lower position) contributes more to RRF score.\n        // shard0: [low_rank_doc (rank 0), mid_rank_doc (rank 10)]\n        // shard1: [high_rank_doc (rank 0)]\n        // shard2: [mid_rank_doc (rank 0) - same doc appears in two shards!]\n        //\n        // Expected RRF scores:\n        // - low_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard0)\n        // - high_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard1)\n        // - mid_rank_doc: 1/(60+10+1) + 1/(60+0+1) = 1/71 + 1/61 (rank 10 in shard0, rank 0 in shard2)\n        //\n        // mid_rank_doc should win because it appears in multiple shards.\n\n        let mut shard0_hits = vec![];\n        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n        let mut shard2_hits = vec![];\n\n        // Build shard0: low_rank_doc at position 0, mid_rank_doc at position 10\n        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"low_rank_doc\", 0.1, 0));\n        for i in 0..9 {\n            shard0_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_0_{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n        }\n        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.2, 0));\n\n        // shard1: high_rank_doc at position 0\n        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"high_rank_doc\", 0.3, 1));\n\n        // shard2: mid_rank_doc at position 0 (same doc appears again!)\n        shard2_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.4, 2));\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 100, 10),\n                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 100, 10),\n                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 100, 10),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // mid_rank_doc should be first because it appears in multiple shards\n        // (RRF contributions sum: rank 10 in shard0 + rank 0 in shard2)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"mid_rank_doc\");\n\n        // low_rank_doc and high_rank_doc both at rank 0 in their shards\n        // Tie-break by primary key alphabetically\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"high_rank_doc\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"low_rank_doc\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_rrf_duplicate_handling() {\n        // Test that the same document appearing in multiple shards\n        // gets its RRF score summed.\n        //\n        // doc1 appears at rank 0 in shard0 and rank 5 in shard1\n        // doc2 appears at rank 0 in shard2\n        //\n        // RRF(doc1) = 1/(60+0+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 1/61 + 1/66\n        // RRF(doc2) = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61\n        //\n        // doc1 should rank higher.\n\n        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)];\n        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n        let shard2_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 2)];\n\n        // Add filler hits to shard1 to make doc1 appear at rank 5\n        for i in 0..5 {\n            shard1_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_1_{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n        }\n        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.2, 1));\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // doc1 should rank higher despite lower score because\n        // it appears in multiple shards and gets RRF contribution boost\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_offset_limit() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc4\", 0.6, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc5\", 0.5, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 1,\n            limit: 2,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_preserves_score_when_requested() {\n        // RRF doesn't use scores for ranking, but we still preserve\n        // the original score field when requested.\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.9)\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_rrf_ignores_scores() {\n        // Test that RRF ordering is based on rank, not score.\n        // Even though doc3 has highest score, it's ranked lower in its shard.\n        //\n        // shard0: doc2 at rank 0 with score 0.9 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n        // shard1: doc1 at rank 0 with score 0.1 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n        // shard2: doc3 at rank 2 (position 2) with score 1.0 (lower rank)\n        //\n        // RRF scores:\n        // - doc1: 1/61 (rank 0)\n        // - doc2: 1/61 (rank 0)\n        // - filler: 1/62 (rank 1)\n        // - doc3: 1/63 (rank 2)\n        //\n        // Ordering: doc1, doc2 (tie-break alphabetically), filler, then doc3\n\n        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 0)]; // High score, rank 0\n        let shard1_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)]; // Low score, rank 0\n        let shard2_hits = vec![\n            make_hit(\"filler\", 0.5, 2),\n            make_hit(\"filler2\", 0.5, 2),\n            make_hit(\"doc3\", 1.0, 2),  // Highest score, but rank 2\n        ];\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // doc1 and doc2 both at rank 0, tie-break alphabetically\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        // filler and filler2 at rank 1\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler2\");\n        // doc3 at rank 2, comes last despite highest score\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[4].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_strips_miroir_fields() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![json!({\n                    \"id\": \"doc1\",\n                    \"title\": \"Test\",\n                    \"_rankingScore\": 0.9,\n                    \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n                    \"_miroir_node\": \"node-1\",\n                    \"_miroir_group\": 1,\n                })],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        let hit = &result.hits[0];\n\n        assert!(hit.get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n        assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n        assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_node\").is_none());\n        assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_group\").is_none());\n\n        // Non-reserved fields preserved.\n        assert_eq!(hit.get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(hit.get(\"title\").unwrap(), \"Test\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_facets() {\n        let shard1 = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"category\": {\n                        \"electronics\": 50,\n                        \"books\": 30,\n                    },\n                    \"brand\": {\n                        \"apple\": 20,\n                    },\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let shard2 = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"category\": {\n                        \"electronics\": 40,\n                        \"clothing\": 25,\n                    },\n                    \"brand\": {\n                        \"samsung\": 15,\n                    },\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard1, shard2],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        let facets = result.facet_distribution.unwrap();\n\n        // Check category merging.\n        let category = facets.get(\"category\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(category.get(\"electronics\"), Some(&90));\n        assert_eq!(category.get(\"books\"), Some(&30));\n        assert_eq!(category.get(\"clothing\"), Some(&25));\n\n        // Check brand merging.\n        let brand = facets.get(\"brand\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(brand.get(\"apple\"), Some(&20));\n        assert_eq!(brand.get(\"samsung\"), Some(&15));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_facets_filter() {\n        let shard = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"category\": {\"electronics\": 50},\n
