#[test]\n    fn test_merge_tie_breaking() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"zebra\", 0.5, 0)], 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"apple\", 0.5, 1)], 50, 10),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        // RRF: both docs at rank 0 in their shards, equal RRF scores.\n        // Tie-break by primary key (apple < zebra lexicographically).\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_degraded_flag() {\n        let failed_shard = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"success\": false,\n                \"message\": \"node unavailable\",\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)], 50, 10),\n                failed_shard,\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_stable_serialization() {\n        let shard1 = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"category\": {\"electronics\": 50, \"books\": 30},\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let shard2 = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 1)],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"brand\": {\"apple\": 20},\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard1.clone(), shard2.clone()],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result1 = merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        let result2 = merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // Serialize both to JSON.\n        let json1 = serde_json::to_value(&result1).unwrap();\n        let json2 = serde_json::to_value(&result2).unwrap();\n\n        // Byte-identical.\n        assert_eq!(\n            serde_json::to_vec(&json1).unwrap(),\n            serde_json::to_vec(&json2).unwrap()\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_page_reconstruction() {\n        // Test that pages of 10 reconstruct a single limit=50 result.\n        let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..50 {\n            all_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"doc{:02}\", i), (50 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0));\n        }\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(all_hits, 50, 10)],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 50,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let full_result = merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(full_result.hits.len(), 50);\n\n        // Now fetch in pages of 10 and verify they match.\n        for page in 0..5 {\n            let page_input = MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: input.shard_hits.clone(),\n                offset: page * 10,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: false,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            };\n            let page_result = merge(page_input).unwrap();\n\n            let start = page * 10;\n            let end = start + 10;\n            assert_eq!(\n                page_result.hits,\n                full_result.hits[start..end],\n                \"Page {} mismatch\",\n                page\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // RRF correctness properties\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_cross_shard_replication_boost() {\n        // Key RRF property: a document appearing in multiple shards\n        // gets a higher merged score than a single-shard document,\n        // even if it ranks lower within each shard.\n        //\n        // doc-replicated: rank 5 in shard0, rank 5 in shard1\n        //   RRF = 1/(60+5+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 2/66 ≈ 0.0303\n        // doc-single: rank 0 in shard2\n        //   RRF = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n        //\n        // doc-replicated should rank higher.\n\n        let mut shard0 = vec![];\n        let mut shard1 = vec![];\n        for i in 0..5 {\n            shard0.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler0-{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n            shard1.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler1-{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n        }\n        shard0.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 0));\n        shard1.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 1));\n\n        let shard2 = vec![make_hit(\"doc-single\", 0.99, 2)];\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![\n                    make_shard_response(shard0, 100, 10),\n                    make_shard_response(shard1, 100, 10),\n                    make_shard_response(shard2, 100, 10),\n                ],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 20,\n                client_requested_score: false,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n        let rep_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-replicated\").unwrap();\n        let single_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-single\").unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            rep_pos < single_pos,\n            \"Replicated doc at pos {} should rank above single doc at pos {}\",\n            rep_pos,\n            single_pos\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_immune_to_score_scale() {\n        // RRF uses rank only: scores of 0.001 vs 0.999 don't affect ordering.\n        // Two shards with wildly different score ranges should produce the\n        // same merge as two shards with uniform scores.\n        let shard_a = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [\n                    {\"id\": \"a1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.001},\n                    {\"id\": \"a2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.002},\n                ],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        };\n        let shard_b = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [\n                    {\"id\": \"b1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.999},\n                    {\"id\": \"b2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.998},\n                ],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![shard_a, shard_b],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: false,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // All at rank 0 or 1 — tie-break alphabetically within each rank tier.\n        // rank 0: a1, b1 → sort by id → a1, b1\n        // rank 1: a2, b2 → sort by id → a2, b2\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n        assert_eq!(ids, vec![\"a1\", \"b1\", \"a2\", \"b2\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_deterministic_with_same_input() {\n        // RRF merge is a pure function: same input always produces same output.\n        let shard = make_shard_response(\n            (0..100).map(|i| make_hit(&format!(\"doc{}\", i), (100 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0)).collect(),\n            1000,\n            10,\n        );\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard; 5],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 50,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r1 = strategy.merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        let r2 = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            serde_json::to_vec(&r1).unwrap(),\n            serde_json::to_vec(&r2).unwrap()\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_default_impl() {\n        let via_default: RrfStrategy = Default::default();\n        let via_constructor = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        assert_eq!(via_default.k(), via_constructor.k());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_pk_field_as_primary_key() {\n        let shard = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [{\"pk\": \"doc-pk\", \"title\": \"Test\"}],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        };\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"pk\").unwrap(), \"doc-pk\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_document_equality() {\n        use super::Ordering;\n        let a = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"doc1\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        let b = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"doc1\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(a, b);\n\n        let c = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"doc2\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_ne!(a, c);\n\n        // NaN: both NaN → Equal\n        let nan_a = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: f64::NAN,\n            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        let nan_b = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: f64::NAN,\n            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&nan_b), Ordering::Equal);\n\n        // NaN vs real: NaN is Less\n        let real = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&real), Ordering::Less);\n        assert_eq!(real.cmp(&nan_a), Ordering::Greater);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Score-based merge tests (OP#4 global-IDF)\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_strategy_exists() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        assert_eq!(strategy.name(), \"score\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_basic() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                15,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 100);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_global_sorting() {\n        // Test that score-based merge sorts globally by score.\n        // With global IDF (simulated here by consistent scores across shards),\n        // doc with highest score should rank first regardless of shard.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                // Shard 0: low scores\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-1\", 0.3, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-2\", 0.2, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n                // Shard 1: high scores (these should rank higher)\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-1\", 0.9, 1),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-2\", 0.8, 1),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 4);\n\n        // Should be sorted by score descending globally\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_tie_breaking() {\n        // Test deterministic tie-breaking on primary key when scores are equal.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"zebra\", 0.5, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"apple\", 0.5, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        // Both docs have same score, should tie-break alphabetically\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_offset_limit() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc4\", 0.6, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc5\", 0.5, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 1,\n            limit: 2,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.9)\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_strips_score_when_not_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test: skewed corpus scenario with global-IDF preflight.\n    ///\n    /// This simulates the scenario described in P12.OP4:\n    /// - Shard 0 has 1000 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 100 (df=100)\n    /// - Shard 1 has 100 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 50 (df=50)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF:\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 0) = log((1000-100+0.5)/(100+0.5)+1) ≈ 2.2\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 1) = log((100-50+0.5)/(50+0.5)+1) ≈ 0.7\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF:\n    /// - Global N = 1100, global df = 150\n    /// - Global IDF = log((1100-150+0.5)/(150+0.5)+1) ≈ 1.8\n    ///\n    /// A document with tf=3 for \"rust\" in each shard:\n    /// - Shard 0 score (local IDF): ~3 * 2.2 = 6.6\n    /// - Shard 1 score (local IDF): ~3 * 0.7 = 2.1\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF, shard 0 doc ranks higher despite shard 1 having\n    /// much higher term density (50/100 vs 100/1000).\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF, both shards use IDF=1.8, and the doc with higher\n    /// term density (normalized by document length) ranks correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_skewed_corpus_integration() {\n        // Simulate global IDF applied (scores are now comparable)\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Doc in large shard with high term frequency but low density\n        let doc_large_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-large\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.75, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // Doc in small shard with lower term frequency but high density\n        let doc_small_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-small\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.85, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // With global IDF, the small shard doc should rank higher\n        // because its term density is higher (50/100 vs 100/1000)\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_large_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_small_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n\n        // Small shard doc should rank first (higher score after global IDF)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-small\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-large\");\n\n        // Scores should be preserved\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.85)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.75)\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test that demonstrates the failure mode without global IDF.\n    ///\n    /// This shows what happens when scores are NOT comparable across shards:\n    /// score-based merge produces incorrect rankings, while RRF at least\n    /// produces consistent (though not optimal) results.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_without_global_idf_fails() {\n        // Simulate the bug: shard-local IDF produces incomparable scores\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Shard 0: large shard, inflated local IDF (shard has term rarity)\n        let doc_shard0 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-inflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95, // Inflated due to high local IDF\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: small shard, deflated local IDF (term is common here)\n        let doc_shard1 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-deflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.60, // Deflated due to low local IDF\n        });\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard0],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 10000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard1],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // Without global IDF, score-based merge trusts the inflated scores\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-inflated\");\n\n        // This is WRONG: doc-deflated has much higher term density but\n        // ranks lower due to shard-local IDF skew.\n        //\n        // The solution is the preflight phase (dfs_query_then_fetch_search)\n        // which computes global IDF so scores are comparable.\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_default_impl() {\n        let via_default: ScoreMergeStrategy = Default::default();\n        let via_constructor = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        assert_eq!(via_default.name(), via_constructor.name());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_empty_input() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 0);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // P12.OP4 RRF skew validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// Validates the P12.OP4 finding: RRF merge with extreme shard skew\n    /// produces incorrect global rankings because it gives equal weight\n    /// to all shards regardless of their size.\n    ///\n    /// Scenario: 10 shards where shard 0 has 93K docs (93%) and shard 9\n    /// has 10 docs (0.01%). RRF assigns identical scores to rank-0 hits\n    /// from all shards, so a mediocre hit from the tiny shard ranks\n    /// equally with the best hit from the dominant shard.\n    ///\n    /// Benchmark result (10K queries, skewed corpus):\n    ///   Score merge:   τ = 0.79  (95% CI [0.787, 0.801]) — FAIL\n    ///   RRF merge:     τ = 0.14  (95% CI [0.134, 0.140]) — FAIL\n    ///   DFS preflight: τ = 0.98  (95% CI [0.982, 0.982]) — PASS\n    ///\n    /// Conclusion: RRF alone does NOT solve cross-shard comparability.\n    /// Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch) is required.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_equal_weight_problem() {\n        // Shard 0 (dominant): doc-best should be the global #1 result.\n        // It has the highest score and appears in the shard with 93% of docs.\n        let shard_dominant = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-best\", 0.95, 0),      // True global #1\n                make_hit(\"doc-good\", 0.90, 0),       // True global #2\n                make_hit(\"doc-ok\", 0.85, 0),         // True global #3\n                make_hit(\"doc-mediocre\", 0.70, 0),   // True global #4\n                make_hit(\"doc-weak\", 0.60, 0),       // True global #5\n            ],\n            93_000,\n            10,\n        );\n\n        // Shard 9 (tiny, 10 docs): due to local IDF skew, irrelevant docs\n        // can appear at rank 0 with inflated local scores.\n        let shard_tiny = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-irrelevant\", 0.98, 9), // Inflated local IDF → high score\n                make_hit(\"doc-noise\", 0.92, 9),\n            ],\n            10,\n            2,\n        );\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![shard_dominant, shard_tiny],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()))\n            .collect();\n\n        // RRF gives equal rank weight to both shards.\n        // Rank 0 from dominant shard: 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n        // Rank 0 from tiny shard:     1/61 ≈ 0.0164 (identical!)\n        //\n        // Tie-breaking falls to primary key (alphabetical), NOT relevance.\n        // doc-best and doc-irrelevant both get RRF score 1/61.\n        // Alphabetically: doc-best < doc-irrelevant → doc-best wins the tie.\n        //\n        // But doc-irrelevant still ranks above doc-good, doc-ok, doc-mediocre,\n        // and doc-weak — all of which are more relevant globally.\n        assert_eq!(ids[0], \"doc-best\");      // Tie-break win (alphabetical)\n        assert_eq!(ids[1], \"doc-irrelevant\"); // Tie-break loss, but still rank 2!\n\n        // doc-irrelevant (globally irrelevant) ranks ABOVE doc-good (global #2)\n        let irrelevant_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-irrelevant\").unwrap();\n        let good_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-good\").unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            irrelevant_pos < good_pos,\n            \"RRF skew bug: irrelevant doc (pos {}) ranks above doc-good (pos {})\",\n            irrelevant_pos,\n            good_pos,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Computes Kendall tau between two rankings (document ID lists).\n    /// Used to validate merge quality against ground truth.\n    fn kendall_tau(ranking1: &[String], ranking2: &[String]) -> f64 {\n        let pos1: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking1\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n        let pos2: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking2\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n\n        let common: Vec<&str> = pos1\n            .keys()\n            .filter(|k| pos2.contains_key(*k))\n            .map(|k| *k)\n            .collect();\n\n        if common.len() < 2 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n\n        let r2_positions: Vec<usize> = common.iter().map(|id| pos2[id]).collect();\n        let (_, discordant) = count_inversions(&r2_positions);\n        let n = common.len();\n        let total = n * (n - 1) / 2;\n        let concordant = total - discordant;\n        (concordant as f64 - discordant as f64) / total as f64\n    }\n\n    fn count_inversions(arr: &[usize]) -> (Vec<usize>, usize) {\n        if arr.len() <= 1 {\n            return (arr.to_vec(), 0);\n        }\n        let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n        let (left, inv_l) = count_inversions(&arr[..mid]);\n        let (right, inv_r) = count_inversions(&arr[mid..]);\n\n        let mut merged = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());\n        let mut inv = inv_l + inv_r;\n        let (mut i, mut j) = (0, 0);\n\n        while i < left.len() && j < right.len() {\n            if left[i] <= right[j] {\n                merged.push(left[i]);\n                i += 1;\n            } else {\n                merged.push(right[j]);\n                inv += left.len() - i;\n                j += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&left[i..]);\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&right[j..]);\n        (merged, inv)\n    }\n\n    /// End-to-end validation: RRF merge on skewed shards produces τ < 0.95\n    /// against ground truth (single-index ranking).\n    ///\n    /// This is a scaled-down version of the 10K-query Python benchmark.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_tau_below_threshold() {\n        let k = DEFAULT_RRF_K;\n\n        // Build 5 shards with skewed sizes: [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000]\n        // Ground truth: all 17600 docs in one index, sorted by score.\n        let mut all_docs: Vec<(String, f64)> = Vec::new();\n        let mut shard_docs: Vec<Vec<(String, f64)>> = vec![vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![]];\n        let shard_sizes = [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000];\n\n        let mut rng = simple_rng(42);\n        for (shard_id, &size) in shard_sizes.iter().enumerate() {\n            for i in 0..size {\n                // Deterministic pseudo-random scores\n                let score = fake_bm25_score(shard_id, i, &mut rng);\n                let doc_id = format!(\"s{}-d{:06}\", shard_id, i);\n                all_docs.push((doc_id.clone(), score));\n                shard_docs[shard_id].push((doc_id, score));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Ground truth: global sort by score descending\n        all_docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        let ground_truth: Vec<String> = all_docs.iter().take(100).map(|(id, _)| id.clone()).collect();\n\n        // Per-shard: sort locally (simulates local BM25 with local IDF)\n        for docs in &mut shard_docs {\n            docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        }\n\n        // RRF merge using the actual Rust merger\n        let shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_docs\n            .iter()\n            .map(|docs| {\n                let hits: Vec<Value> = docs\n                    .iter()\n                    .take(200)\n                    .map(|(id, score)| {\n                        json!({\n                            \"id\": id,\n                            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n                        })\n                    })\n                    .collect();\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": hits,\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": docs.len(),\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(k);\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: shard_pages,\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 100,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let rrf_ranking: Vec<String> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from))\n            .collect();\n\n        let tau = kendall_tau(&ground_truth, &rrf_ranking);\n\n        // RRF with skewed shards should produce τ well below 0.95.\n        assert!(\n            tau < 0.95,\n            \"RRF tau = {:.4}, expected < 0.95 with skewed shards\",\n            tau,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Simple deterministic PRNG for reproducible test scores.\n    fn simple_rng(seed: u64) -> impl FnMut() -> f64 {\n        let mut state = seed;\n        move || {\n            state = state.wrapping_mul(6_364_136_223_846_793_005).wrapping_add(1);\n            (state >> 33) as f64 / (1u64 << 31) as f64\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Simulates a BM25-like score with shard-dependent IDF skew.\n    fn fake_bm25_score(shard_id: usize, _doc_idx: usize, rng: &mut impl FnMut() -> f64) -> f64 {\n        let tf = 1.0 + rng() * 10.0;\n        // Larger shards have lower IDF for common terms (simulating skew)\n        let shard_weight = match shard_id {\n            0 => 0.3,\n            1 => 0.5,\n            2 => 0.7,\n            3 => 0.9,\n            4 => 1.0,\n            _ => 0.5,\n        };\n        tf * shard_weight + rng() * 0.5\n    }\n}\n","numLines":2018,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2018}}}

&PreflightRequest,\n383\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n384\t        let start = Instant::now();\n385\t        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n386\t\n387\t        tracing::debug!(\n388\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n389\t            node_id = %node,\n390\t            address = %address,\n391\t            index = %request.index_uid,\n392\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n393\t            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n394\t            \"node call started\"\n395\t        );\n396\t\n397\t        // 1. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n455\t        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n456\t        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n457\t\n458\t        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n459\t        tracing::debug!(\n460\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n461\t            node_id = %node,\n462\t            address = %address,\n463\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n464\t            duration_ms,\n465\t            total_docs,\n466\t            \"node call completed\"\n467\t        );\n468\t\n469\t        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n470\t            total_docs,\n471\t            avg_doc_length,\n472\t            term_stats,\n473\t        })\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    fn get_task_status(\n477\t        &self,\n478\t        node: &NodeId,\n479\t        address: &str,\n480\t .timeout(Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self { client, master_key }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the search URL the preflight URL for a node and index.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    fn preflight_url(&self, address: &str, index_uid: &str) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/_preflight\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid)\n    }\n\n    /// Build the documents URL for a node and index.\n    fn documents_url(&self, address: &str, index_uid: &str) -> String {\n        format!(\n task_url_static(address: &str, task_uid: u64) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}/tasks/{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), task_uid)\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for HttpClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"node_call\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"search\",\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n        );\n        let _guard = span.enter();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.search_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        let mut body = request.to_node_body();\n\n        if let Some(global_idf) = &request.global_idf {\n            body[\"_miroir_global_idf\"] = serde_json::to_value(global_idf)\n                .map_err(|e| .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    target: \"miroir.node\",\n                    duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"node call failed\"\n                );\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n        if NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.documents_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"write_documents\",\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        let mut query_params = Vec::new();\n        if let Some(pk) = &request.primary_key {\n            query_params.push((\"primaryKey\", pk.as_str()));\n        }\n\n        let mut req_builder = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.documents);\n\n        if !query_params.is_empty() {\n            req_builder = req_builder.query(&query_params);\n        }\n\n {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n    });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n operation = \"write_documents\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start =            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.ids)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"delete_documents\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(DeleteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    code: meili_err.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents/delete\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            request.index_uid\n        );\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"delete_by_filter\",\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.filter)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"delete_by_filter\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(DeleteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n        let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n        let stats_resp = self\n            .client\n            .get(&stats_url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n            // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n            return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                total_docs: 0,\n                avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n                term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_docs = stats_body\n            .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n        for term in &request.terms {\n            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n\n            let search_resp = self\n                .client\n                .post(&search_url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n                .json(&search_body)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n                let body: Value = search_resp\n                    .json()\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n                let df = body\n                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            duration_ms,\n            total_docs,\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs,\n            avg_doc_length,\n            term_stats,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let task_uid = request.task_uid;\n        let url = Self::task_url_static(address, task_uid);\n        let master_key = self.master_key.clone();\n        let client = self.client.clone();\n\n        async move {\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response\n                .text()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                    status: status.as_u16(),\n                    body: body_text,\n                });\n            }\n\n            // Parse successful response\n           .to_string(),\n                error: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"message\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                error_type: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"type\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_url_construction() {\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\"test-key\".into(), 5000);\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.search_url(\"http://localhost:7700\", \"my_index\"),\n            \"http://localhost:7700/indexes/my_index/search\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.search_url(\"http://localhost:7700/\", \"my_index\"),\n When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;\n101\t    }\n102\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n106\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n107\t    (0..shard_count)\n108\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n109\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n110\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n111\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n112\t        })\n113\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n114\t        .into_iter()\n115\t        .collect()\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n119\t///\n120\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n121\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n122\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n123\t    shard_count: u32,\n124\t    group: &Group,\n125\t    rf: usize,\n126\t    query_seq: u64,\n127\t    index: &str,\n128\t    floor: u64,\n129\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n130\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n131\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n132\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n133\t\n134\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n136\t\n137\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n138\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n139\t            .iter()\n140\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n141\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n142\t                version >= floor\n143\t            })\n144\t            .collect();\n145\t\n146\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n147\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n148\t            return None;\n149\t        }\n150\t\n151\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n152\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n153\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n154\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    Some(result)\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n162\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n163\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n164\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n165\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n169\t///\n170\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n171\t/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\n172\tpub fn count_assignment_diff(\n173\t    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n174\t    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n175\t) -> usize {\n176\t    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n177\t        .iter()\n178\t        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n179\t        .collect();\n180\t\n181\t    let mut diff = 0;\n182\t    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n183\t        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n184\t            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n185\t            for node in new_nodes {\n186\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n187\t                    diff += 1;\n188\t                }\n189\t            }\n190\t        }\n191\t    }\n192\t    diff\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t#[cfg(test)]\n196\tmod tests {\n197\t    use super::*;\n198\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n199\t    use std::collections::HashMap;\n200\t\n201\t    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n202\t    #[test]\n203\t    fn test_determinism() {\n204\t        let nodes = ];\n209\t\n210\t        let reference = (0..100)\n211\t            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n212\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n213\t\n214\t        // Run 1000 times and compare to reference\n215\t        for _ in 0..1000 {\n216\t            let current = (0..100)\n217\t                .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id,  /// Test tie-breaking in assign_shard_in_group.\n399\t    #[test]\n400\t    fn test_tie_breaking() {\n401\t        // Use node IDs that are unlikely but could theoretically collide\n402\t        let &nodes, 1);\n414\t        assert_eq!(result, result2, \"Tie-breaking is non-deterministic\");\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    /// Test score function uses canonical hash order.\n418\t    #[test]\n419\t    fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n420\t        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n421\t        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n422\t\n423\t        // Different shard_id → different score\n424\t        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n425\t        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n426\t        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n427\t\n428\t        // differ\");\n431\t\n432\t        // Verify determinism\n433\t        assert_eq!(score(0, node_a), score_0_a, \"Score is non-deterministic\");\n434\t    }\n435\t\n436\t    // ── P1.3 acceptance tests:     for i in 0u32..6 {\n567\t            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n568\t            let node = Node::new(\n569\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n570\t P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n625\t            chi_square,\n626\t            critical_value\n627\t        );\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set rotates replicas by query_seq.\n631\t    #[test]\n632\t    fn test_covering_set_rotates_replicas() {\n633\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(8, 1, 3);\n634\t        for i in 0u32..4 {\n635\t            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n636\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n637\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n638\t                0,\n639\t            ));\n640\t        }\n641\t\n642\t        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n643\t        let c0 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 0);\n644\t        let c1 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 1);\n645\t        let c2 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n646\t\n647\t        // For each shard, verify that the selected node rotates\n648\t        for shard_id in 0..8 {\n649\t            let replicas = assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n657\t        }\n658\t    }\n659\t}\n660\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c492678-e3a6-4eb7-8039-c2c30e309afc","uuid":"518e0733-0b4f-4631-9f06-4e80cbe6a99c","timestamp":"2026-05-23T18:01:07.180Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n/// for deterministic resolution.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n///\n/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\npub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n    shard_id: u32,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n\n    // Start with standard write targets\n    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n\n    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n\n                    // Only add if not already in targets\n                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    targets\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n///\n/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    if replica_groups == 0 {\n        return 0;\n    }\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n///\n/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\npub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n    shard_count: u32,\n    group: &Group,\n    rf: usize,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    index: &str,\n    floor: u64,\n    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n    let mut result = Vec::new();\n    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n                version >= floor\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            // No eligible replica for this shard\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n            result.push(selected.clone());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Some(result)\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n///\n/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\npub fn count_assignment_diff(\n    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n) -> usize {\n    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n        .iter()\n        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut diff = 0;\n    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n            for node in new_nodes {\n                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n                    diff += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    diff\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_determinism() {\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let reference = (0..100)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n        // Run 1000 times and compare to reference\n        for _ in 0..1000 {\n            let current = (0..100)\n                .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n            assert_eq!(reference, current, \"Assignment is non-deterministic\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test 2: Reshuffle bound on add — 64 shards, 3→4 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 shard-node edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut nodes_4 = nodes_3.clone();\n        nodes_4.push(NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()));\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n\n        assert!(\n            diff <= max_diff,\n            \"Add reshuffle exceeded bound: {} > {}\",\n            diff,\n            max_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 3: Reshuffle bound on remove — 64 shards, 4→3 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: ~RF × S / Ng edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n        let shard_count = NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id,   }\n\n    /// Test 6: shard_for_key matches known fixture.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n        // Known test vectors computed with: XxHash64::with_seed(0).hash(pk) % assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n\n        // Even if scores were identical (extremely rare), node-aaa should come first\n        // because we sort by node_id ascending for ties\n assert_eq!(result, result2, \"Tie-breaking is non-deterministic\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test score function uses canonical hash order.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n\n        // Different shard_id → different score\n        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n\n        // Different node_id → different score (same shard)\n        let score_0_b = score(0, node_b);\n        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_0_b, \"Same shard, different node should differ\");\n\n        // Verify determinism\n        assert_eq!(score(0, node_a), score_0_a, \"Score is non-deterministic\");\n    }\n\n    // ── P1.3 acceptance tests: write_targets, query_group, covering_set ───\n\n    /// P1.3-A1: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes (counting duplicates).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_returns_rg_x_rf_nodes() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 3, 2);\n        // Add 5 nodes to each of 3 groups\n        for i in 0u32..15 {\n            let rg = i / 5;\n   /// P1.3-A2: write_targets assigns one-per-group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_one_per_group() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: nodes 0-2, Group 1: nodes 3-5\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n \"Group 1 missing expected node\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A3: covering_set covers all shards within the chosen group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_covers_all_shards() {\n uniform (chi-square test).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n        let samples = 10_000;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n\n        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n            .iter()\n            .map(|&observed| {\n                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n                (diff * diff) / expected\n            })\n            .sum();\n\n        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n        let critical_value = 9.49;\n\n        assert!(\n            chi_square < critical_value,\n            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n            chi_square,\n            critical_value\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set Execute Scatter (`execute_scatter`)\n- **Location**: `crates/miroir-core/src/scatter.rs:598-742`\n- **Logic**: Fans out search requests to all nodes in covering set\n- **Features**:\n  - Parallel requests with `join_all`\n  - Unavailable shard policies: Partial, Error, Fallback\n  - Group fallback on failure (plan §2)\n- **Status**: ✅ Implemented\n\n### 5. Request Transformation (`SearchRequest::to_node_body`)\n- **Location**: `crates/miroir-core/src/scatter.rs:341-376`\n- **Logic**: Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally\n- **Pagination**: Sets `limit = offset + limit`, `offset = 0`\n- **Status**: ✅ Implemented\n\n### 6. Merge (`scatter_gather_search`, `merge`)\n- **Location**: `crates/miroir-core/src/scatter.rs:745-785`, `crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs`\n- **Strategies**:\n  - RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) - default\n  - Score-based (for OP#4 global-IDF)\n- **Features**:\n  - Deduplication by primary key\n  - Facet aggregation (sum across shards)\n  - `estimatedTotalHits` sum\n  - `processingTimeMs` max\n- **Status**: ✅ Implemented\n\n### 7. HTTP Endpoint (`search_handler`)\n- **Location**: `crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs:163-686`\n- **Route**: `POST /search/:index`\n- **Features**:\n  - DFS query-then-fetch (OP#4 global-IDF preflight)\n  - Session pinning (plan §13.6)\n  - Query coalescing (plan §13.10)\n  - X-Miroir-Degraded header\n  - Settings version headers\n- **Status**: ✅ Implemented\n\n### 8.
